translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: HA MIM.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Ha-Meem. (Alphabets of the Arabic language – Allah, and to whomever He reveals, know their precise meanings.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Ha Mim

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Ha-Mim.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Ha-Mim. [These letters are one of the miracles of the Quran, and none but Allah (Alone) knows their meanings.]

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Ha, Meem.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Ha'. Mim.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Ha Mim.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Ha. Mim.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Ha, Meem.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: HaMeem.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Ha, Meem.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Ha. Mim.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Ha Mim!

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Ha Mim.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Ha Mim:

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Hameem

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: A revelation from the most benevolent, ever-merciful,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: This (Qur’an) is sent down from the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: A sending down from the Merciful, the Compassionate.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: This is a revelation from the Compassionate, the Merciful -

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: A revelation from Allah, the Most Beneficent, the Most Merciful.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: A revelation from the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: This is a revelation from the Most Merciful, the Most Compassionate,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: A revelation from the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: A revelation from the Beneficent, the Merciful,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: A [gradually] sent down [revelation] from the All-beneficent, the All-merciful,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: A sending down from the Merciful, the Most Merciful.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [This is] a revelation from the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful -

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: This is the revelations from the Beneficent, Merciful God.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: A revelation from the Beneficent, the Merciful Allah:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: A revelation from [God], the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful --

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: A Revelation from (Allah), Most Gracious, Most Merciful;-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Tanzeelun mina alrrahmani alrraheemi

3.arabic: كِتَـٰبٌ فُصِّلَتْ ءَايَـٰتُهُۥ قُرْءَانًا عَرَبِيًّا لِّقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: A Book whose verses have been distinguished and explained, a lucid discourse for people who understand,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: A Book the verses of which are explained in detail, the Qur’an in Arabic for people of intellect.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: A Book whose signs have been distinguished as an Arabic Koran for a people having knowledge,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: A Book whereof the verses are detailed: an Arabic Qur'an: for a people who know:

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: A Book whereof the Verses are explained in detail; A Quran in Arabic for people who know.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: A Scripture whose Verses are detailed, a Quran in Arabic for people who know.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: a Book whose verses have been well-expounded; an Arabic Qur'an for those who have knowledge,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: A Book whereof the Ayat are explained in detail -- a Qur'an in Arabic for people who know.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: A Scripture whereof the verses are expounded, a Lecture in Arabic for people who have knowledge,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: [this is] a Book whose signs have been elaborated for a people who have knowledge, an Arabic Quran,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: A Book, the verses of which are distinguished, an Arabic Koran for a nation who know.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: A Book whose verses have been detailed, an Arabic Qur'an for a people who know,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The verses of this Book have been fully expounded. It is a reading in the Arabic language for the people of knowledge.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: A Book of which the verses are made plain, an Arabic Quran for a people who know:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: a Book whose revelations are well expounded, an Arabic Quran for people who possess knowledge,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: A Book, whereof the verses are explained in detail;- a Qur'an in Arabic, for people who understand;-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Kitabun fussilat ayatuhu quranan AAarabiyyan liqawmin yaAAlamoona

4.arabic: بَشِيرًا وَنَذِيرًا فَأَعْرَضَ أَكْثَرُهُمْ فَهُمْ لَا يَسْمَعُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Announcing happy news and warnings. And yet most of them are averse and do not listen,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Giving glad tidings and heralding warning; so most of them turned away, therefore they cannot listen (to the truth).

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: good tidings to bear, and warning, but most of them have turned away, and do not give ear.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: A bearer of glad tidings and a warner. Yet most of them turn aside, so that they hearken not.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Giving glad tidings [of Paradise to the one who believes in the Oneness of Allah (i.e. Islamic Monotheism) and fears Allah much (abstains from all kinds of sins and evil deeds) and loves Allah much (performing all kinds of good deeds which He has ordained)], and warning (of punishment in the Hell Fire to the one who disbelieves in the Oneness of Allah), but most of them turn away, so they listen not.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Bringing good news, and giving warnings. But most of them turn away, so they do not listen.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: one bearing good news and warning. Yet most of them turned away and are not wont to give heed.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Giving glad tidings and warning, but most of them turn away, so they hear not.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Good tidings and a warning. But most of them turn away so that they hear not.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: a bearer of good news and a warner. But most of them turn away [from it], [and] so they do not listen.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: It bears glad tidings and a warning, yet most of them turn away and do not listen.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: As a giver of good tidings and a warner; but most of them turn away, so they do not hear.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: It contains glad news and warnings (for the people), but most of them have ignored it and do not listen.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: A herald of good news and a warner, but most of them turn aside so they hear not.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: proclaiming good news and a warning. Yet most of them turn away and so do not listen.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Giving good news and admonition: yet most of them turn away, and so they hear not.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Basheeran wanatheeran faaAArada aktharuhum fahum la yasmaAAoona

5.arabic: وَقَالُوا۟ قُلُوبُنَا فِىٓ أَكِنَّةٍ مِّمَّا تَدْعُونَآ إِلَيْهِ وَفِىٓ ءَاذَانِنَا وَقْرٌ وَمِنۢ بَيْنِنَا وَبَيْنِكَ حِجَابٌ فَٱعْمَلْ إِنَّنَا عَـٰمِلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And say: "Our hearts are immured against what you call us to. There is a deafness in our ears, and a veil lies between us and you. So act (your way), we are acting (ours)."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And they say, “Our hearts are covered against the affair you call us to, and there is deafness in our ears, and there is a barrier between us and you – therefore mind your own business, we are minding ours.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They say, 'Our hearts are veiled from what thou callest us to, and in our ears is a heaviness, and between us and thee there is a veil; so act; we are acting!'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And they say: our hearts are under veils from that whereUnto thou callest us, and in our ears there is heaviness, and betwixt us and thee there is a curtain; work thou then, verily we are Workers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And they say: "Our hearts are under coverings (screened) from that to which you invite us, and in our ears is deafness, and between us and you is a screen, so work you (on your way); verily, we are working (on our way)."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And they say, “Our hearts are screened from what you call us to, and in our ears is deafness, and between us and you is a barrier. So do what you want, and so will we.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They say: “Our hearts are securely wrapped up against what you call us to, and in our ears is a heaviness, and between you and us there is a veil. So act; we too are acting.”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And they say: "Our hearts are under coverings from that to which you invite us; and in our ears is deafness, and between us and you is a screen, so work you (on your way); verily, we are working (on our way)."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And they say: Our hearts are protected from that unto which thou (O Muhammad) callest us, and in our ears there is a deafness, and between us and thee there is a veil. Act, then. Lo! we also shall be acting.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They say, ‘Our hearts are in veils [which shut them off] from what you invite us to, and there is a deafness in our ears, and there is a curtain between us and you. So act [as your faith requires]; we too are acting [according to our own].’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They say: 'Our hearts are veiled from that to which you call us, and in our ears there is heaviness. And between us and you is a veil. So work (as you will) and we are working'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And they say, "Our hearts are within coverings from that to which you invite us, and in our ears is deafness, and between us and you is a partition, so work; indeed, we are working."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They say, "Our hearts are covered against and our ears are deaf to whatever you (Muhammad) invite us to. There is a barrier between us and you. So act as you please and we shall act as we please".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And they say: Our hearts are under coverings from that to which you call us, and there is a heaviness in our ears, and a veil hangs between us and you, so work, we too are working.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: And they say, "Our hearts are encased against that to which you call us, and there is a heaviness in our ears, and there is a barrier between us, so do as you will and so shall we."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They say: "Our hearts are under veils, (concealed) from that to which thou dost invite us, and in our ears in a deafness, and between us and thee is a screen: so do thou (what thou wilt); for us, we shall do (what we will!)"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waqaloo quloobuna fee akinnatin mimma tadAAoona ilayhi wafee athanina waqrun wamin baynina wabaynika hijabun faiAAmal innana AAamiloona

6.arabic: قُلْ إِنَّمَآ أَنَا۠ بَشَرٌ مِّثْلُكُمْ يُوحَىٰٓ إِلَىَّ أَنَّمَآ إِلَـٰهُكُمْ إِلَـٰهٌ وَٰحِدٌ فَٱسْتَقِيمُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِ وَٱسْتَغْفِرُوهُ ۗ وَوَيْلٌ لِّلْمُشْرِكِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Say: "I am a man like you, (but) it is revealed to me that your God is one God, so take the straight path to Him, and ask Him to forgive your sins. Woe to the idolaters

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Proclaim (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “Physically I am a human like you – I receive the divine revelation that your God is only One God, therefore be upright towards Him and seek forgiveness from Him”; and woe is to the polytheists. –

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Say: 'I am only a mortal, like you are. To me it has been revealed that your God is One God; so go straight with Him, and ask for His forgiveness; and woe to the idolaters

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Say thou: I am only a human being like Unto you: only it is revealed Unto me that your God is but One God, wherefore take the straight path Unto Him and seek forgiveness of Him. And woe be Unto the associators.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "I am only a human being like you. It is inspired in me that your Ilah (God) is One Ilah (God - Allah), therefore take Straight Path to Him (with true Faith Islamic Monotheism) and obedience to Him, and seek forgiveness of Him. And woe to Al-Mushrikun (the disbelievers in the Oneness of Allah, polytheists, idolaters, etc. - see V. 2:105).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Say, “I am only a human like you; it is inspired in me that your God is One God. So be upright towards Him, and seek forgiveness from Him.” And woe to the idolaters.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Tell them, (O Prophet): “I am only a human being like you. It is revealed to me that your God is One God; so direct yourselves straight to Him, and seek His forgiveness. Woe to those who associate others with Allah in His Divinity,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Say: "I am only a human being like you. It is revealed to me that your God is One God, therefore take straight path to Him and obedience to Him, and seek forgiveness of Him. And woe to the polytheists.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Say (unto them O Muhammad): I am only a mortal like you. It is inspired in me that your Allah is One Allah, therefor take the straight path unto Him and seek forgiveness of Him. And woe unto the idolaters,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Say, ‘I am just a human like you. It has been revealed to me that your God is the One God. So worship Him single-mindedly and plead to Him for forgiveness.’ And woe to the polytheists

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Say (Prophet Muhammad): 'I am only a human like you, to whom it is revealed that your God is One God. Therefore be straight with Him and ask Him to forgive you. Woe to the idolaters,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Say, O [Muhammad], "I am only a man like you to whom it has been revealed that your god is but one God; so take a straight course to Him and seek His forgiveness." And woe to those who associate others with Allah -

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), say, "I am a mere mortal like you. I have received a revelation that your Lord is the only One. So be up-right and obedient to Him and seek forgiveness from Him.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Say: I am only a mortal like you; it is revealed to me that your Allah is one Allah, therefore follow the right way to Him and ask His forgiveness; and woe to the polytheists;

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Say, "I am only a human being like yourselves. It has been revealed to me that your God is One God. So take the straight path to Him and ask His forgiveness." Woe to those who associate others with Him,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Say thou: "I am but a man like you: It is revealed to me by Inspiration, that your Allah is one Allah: so stand true to Him, and ask for His Forgiveness." And woe to those who join gods with Allah,-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qul innama ana basharun mithlukum yooha ilayya annama ilahukum ilahun wahidun faistaqeemoo ilayhi waistaghfiroohu wawaylun lilmushrikeena

7.arabic: ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْتُونَ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ وَهُم بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ هُمْ كَـٰفِرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Who do not give a due share of their wealth for the welfare of others, and do not believe in the Hereafter.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Those who do not give the obligatory charity, and who deny the Hereafter.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: who pay not the alms, and disbelieve in the world to come.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Who give not the poor-rate; and they! in the Hereafter, they are disbelievers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Those who give not the Zakat and they are disbelievers in the Hereafter.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those who do not pay the alms; and regarding the Hereafter, they are disbelievers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: who do not pay Zakah, and who deny the Hereafter.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Those who give not the Zakah and they are disbelievers in the Hereafter.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Who give not the poor-due, and who are disbelievers in the Hereafter.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: —those who do not pay the zakat and disbelieve in the Hereafter.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: who do not pay charity and disbelieve in the Everlasting Life.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Those who do not give zakah, and in the Hereafter they are disbelievers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Woe to the pagans, who do not pay zakat and have no faith in the life to come.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: (To) those who do not give poor-rate and they are unbelievers in the hereafter.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: who do not pay the zakat, and who deny the Hereafter.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Those who practise not regular Charity, and who even deny the Hereafter.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Allatheena la yutoona alzzakata wahum bialakhirati hum kafiroona

8.arabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ لَهُمْ أَجْرٌ غَيْرُ مَمْنُونٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But those who believe and do the right, will have a continuing reward.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Indeed for those who believed and did good deeds, is a limitless reward.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Surely those who believe, and do righteous deeds shall have a wage unfailing.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily those who believe and work righteous works - Unto them shall be a hire unceasing.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Truly, those who believe (in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism, and in His Messenger Muhammad SAW) and do righteous good deeds, for them will be an endless reward that will never stop (i.e. Paradise).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: As for those who believe and do righteous deeds—for them is a reward uninterrupted.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: As to those who have faith and do good works, surely theirs shall be a never-ending reward.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Truly, those who believe and do righteous good deeds, for them will be an endless reward that will never stop.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! as for those who believe and do good works, for them is a reward enduring.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: As for those who have faith and do righteous deeds, there will be an everlasting reward for them.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: For those who believe and do good works is an enduring wage'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, those who believe and do righteous deeds - for them is a reward uninterrupted.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The righteously striving believers will have a never-ending reward".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: (As for) those who believe and do good, they shall surely have a reward never to be cut off.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: "The ones who believe and perform good deeds shall have a reward which will never be withheld from them."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: For those who believe and work deeds of righteousness is a reward that will never fail.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna allatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati lahum ajrun ghayru mamnoonin

9.arabic: قُلْ أَئِنَّكُمْ لَتَكْفُرُونَ بِٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلْأَرْضَ فِى يَوْمَيْنِ وَتَجْعَلُونَ لَهُۥٓ أَندَادًا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ رَبُّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Say: "Do you refuse to believe in Him who created the earth in two spans of time, and set up compeers to Him, the Lord of all the worlds?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Say “What! You disbelieve in Him Who created the earth in two days, and you appoint equals to Him? He is the Lord Of The Creation!”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Say: 'What, do you disbelieve in Him who created the earth in two days, and do you set up compeers to Him? That is the Lord of all Being.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Say thou: are ye indeed those who disbelieve in Him who hath created the earth in two days, and set up Unto Him peers? That is the Lord of the worlds.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "Do you verily disbelieve in Him Who created the earth in two Days and you set up rivals (in worship) with Him? That is the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Say, “Do you reject the One who created the earth in two days? And you attribute equals to Him? That is the Lord of the Universe.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Tell them, (O Prophet): “Do you indeed disbelieve in Him and assign compeers to Him Who created the earth in two days? He is the Lord of all beings of the Universe.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Say: "Do you verily disbelieve in Him Who created the earth in two Days And you set up rivals with Him That is the Lord of all that exists."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Say (O Muhammad, unto the idolaters): Disbelieve ye verily in Him Who created the earth in two Days, and ascribe ye unto Him rivals? He (and none else) is the Lord of the Worlds.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Say, ‘Do you really disbelieve in Him who created the earth in two days, and ascribe partners to Him? That is the Lord of all the worlds!’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Say: 'Do you disbelieve in Him who created the earth in two days? And do you set up equals with Him? He is the Lord of the Worlds'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Say, "Do you indeed disbelieve in He who created the earth in two days and attribute to Him equals? That is the Lord of the worlds."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Say, "Do you really disbelieve in the One Who created the earth in two days? Do you consider things equal to Him? He is the Lord of the Universe.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Say: What! do you indeed disbelieve in Him Who created the earth in two periods, and do you set up equals with Him? That is the Lord of the Worlds.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Say, "What! Do you indeed deny Him who created the earth in two Days [periods] and do you set up equals with Him? He is the Lord of the Universe."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Say: Is it that ye deny Him Who created the earth in two Days? And do ye join equals with Him? He is the Lord of (all) the Worlds.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qul ainnakum latakfuroona biallathee khalaqa alarda fee yawmayni watajAAaloona lahu andadan thalika rabbu alAAalameena

10.arabic: وَجَعَلَ فِيهَا رَوَٰسِىَ مِن فَوْقِهَا وَبَـٰرَكَ فِيهَا وَقَدَّرَ فِيهَآ أَقْوَٰتَهَا فِىٓ أَرْبَعَةِ أَيَّامٍ سَوَآءً لِّلسَّآئِلِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: He placed firm stabilisers rising above its surface, blessed it with plenty and growth, and ingrained the means of growing its food within it, sufficient for all seekers, in four spans.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And in it He placed mountains as anchors from above it, and blessings in it, and appointed the sustenance for those who dwell in it – all this in four days; a proper answer to those who question.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: 'And He set therein firm mountains over it, and He blessed it, and He ordained therein its diverse sustenance in four days, equal to those who ask.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And He placed therein mountains firmly rooted rising above it, and blessed it, and ordained therein the sustenance thereof, all this in four days: comglete: this for the inquirers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: He placed therein (i.e. the earth) firm mountains from above it, and He blessed it, and measured therein its sustenance (for its dwellers) in four Days equal (i.e. all these four 'days' were equal in the length of time), for all those who ask (about its creation).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He placed stabilizers over it; and blessed it; and planned its provisions in four days, equally to the seekers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (After creating the earth) He set up firm mountains on it, blessed it, and provided it with sustenance in proportion to the needs of all who seek (sustenance). All this was done in four days.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: He placed therein firm mountains from above it, and He blessed it, and measured therein its sustenance in four Days equal for all those who ask.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He placed therein firm hills rising above it, and blessed it and measured therein its sustenance in four Days, alike for (all) who ask;

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He set in it firm mountains, [rising] above it, blessed it, and ordained in it, in four days, [various] means of sustenance, equally for all seekers.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He set firm mountains on top (of the earth) and He blessed it. And in four days He ordained in it many provisions, equal to those who ask.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And He placed on the earth firmly set mountains over its surface, and He blessed it and determined therein its [creatures'] sustenance in four days without distinction - for [the information] of those who ask.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: In four days He placed the mountains on it, blessed it, and equally measured out sustenance for those who seek sustenance.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And He made in it mountains above its surface, and He blessed therein and made therein its foods, in four periods: alike for the seekers.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He placed firm mountains on the earth, and blessed it. He measured out its means of sustenance all in four Days; this is for those who ask for it.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: He set on the (earth), mountains standing firm, high above it, and bestowed blessings on the earth, and measure therein all things to give them nourishment in due proportion, in four Days, in accordance with (the needs of) those who seek (Sustenance).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: WajaAAala feeha rawasiya min fawqiha wabaraka feeha waqaddara feeha aqwataha fee arbaAAati ayyamin sawaan lilssaileena

11.arabic: ثُمَّ ٱسْتَوَىٰٓ إِلَى ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَهِىَ دُخَانٌ فَقَالَ لَهَا وَلِلْأَرْضِ ٱئْتِيَا طَوْعًا أَوْ كَرْهًا قَالَتَآ أَتَيْنَا طَآئِعِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Then He turned to the heavens, and it was smoke. So He said to it and the earth: "Come with willing obedience or perforce." They said: "We come willingly."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: He then inclined towards the heavens and it was smoke – thereupon He said to it and to the earth, “Both of you present yourselves, willingly or with reluctance”; they said, “We present ourselves, with zeal.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Then He lifted Himself to heaven when it was smoke, and said to it and to the earth, "Come willingly, or unwillingly!" They said, "We come willingly."

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Thereafter turned He to the heaven and it was as smoke, and said Unto it and Unto the earth: come ye twain, willingly or loth. They said: we come willingly.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then He Istawa (rose over) towards the heaven when it was smoke, and said to it and to the earth: "Come both of you willingly or unwillingly." They both said: "We come, willingly."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Then He turned to the sky, and it was smoke, and said to it and to the earth, “Come, willingly or unwillingly.” They said, “We come willingly.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Then He turned to the heaven while it was all smoke. He said to the heaven and the earth: “Come (into being), willingly or unwillingly.” They said: “Here we come (into being) in willing obeisance.”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then He rose over (Istawa ila) the heaven when it was smoke, and said to it and to the earth: "Come both of you willingly or unwillingly." They both said: "We come willingly."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Then turned He to the heaven when it was smoke, and said unto it and unto the earth: Come both of you, willingly or loth. They said: We come, obedient.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Then He turned to the heaven, and it was smoke, and He said to it and to the earth, ‘Come, willingly or unwillingly!’ They said, ‘We come obediently.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Then He willed to the heaven when it was smoke, and to it and the earth He said: 'Come willingly, or unwillingly' 'We come willingly' they answered.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then He directed Himself to the heaven while it was smoke and said to it and to the earth, "Come [into being], willingly or by compulsion." They said, "We have come willingly."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He established His dominance over the sky, which (for that time) was like smoke. Then He told the heavens and the earth, "Take your shape either willingly or by force" They said, "We willingly obey".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then He directed Himself to the heaven and it is a vapor, so He said to it and to the earth: Come both, willingly or unwillingly. They both said: We come willingly.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Then He turned to heaven when it was vapour and said to it and to the earth, "Come willingly or unwillingly." They both said, "We come willingly,"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Moreover He comprehended in His design the sky, and it had been (as) smoke: He said to it and to the earth: "Come ye together, willingly or unwillingly." They said: "We do come (together), in willing obedience."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Thumma istawa ila alssamai wahiya dukhanun faqala laha walilardi itiya tawAAan aw karhan qalata atayna taiAAeena

12.arabic: فَقَضَىٰهُنَّ سَبْعَ سَمَـٰوَاتٍ فِى يَوْمَيْنِ وَأَوْحَىٰ فِى كُلِّ سَمَآءٍ أَمْرَهَا ۚ وَزَيَّنَّا ٱلسَّمَآءَ ٱلدُّنْيَا بِمَصَـٰبِيحَ وَحِفْظًا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ تَقْدِيرُ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْعَلِيمِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Then He created several skies in two spans, and ingrained in each sky its function, decking the nearest heaven with lamps, and guarded it. This has been determined by the mighty and all-knowing.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: He then established them into seven heavens in two days, and to each heaven He sent the command of its affairs; and We decorated the lower heaven with lamps; and for its protection; this is the command set by the Most Honourable, the All Knowing.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So He determined them as seven heavens in two days, and revealed its commandment in every heaven.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Then He decreed them as seven heavens in two days, and revealed Unto each heaven the command thereof; and We bedecked the nether heaven with lamps and placed therein a guard. That is the ordinance of the Mighty, the Knower.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then He completed and finished from their creation (as) seven heavens in two Days and He made in each heaven its affair. And We adorned the nearest (lowest) heaven with lamps (stars) to be an adornment as well as to guard (from the devils by using them as missiles against the devils). Such is the Decree of Him the All-Mighty, the All-Knower.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So He completed them as seven universes in two days, and He assigned to each universe its laws. And We decorated the lower universe with lamps, and for protection. That is the design of the Almighty, the All-Knowing.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Then He made them seven heavens in two days and revealed to each heaven its law. And We adorned the lower heaven with lamps, and firmly secured it. All this is the firm plan of the All-Mighty, the All-Knowing.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then He completed and finished their creation (as) seven heavens in two Days and He made in each heaven its affair. And We adorned the nearest (lowest) heaven with lamps (stars) to be an adornment as well as to guard. Such is the decree of Him, the Almighty, the All-Knower.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Then He ordained them seven heavens in two Days and inspired in each heaven its mandate; and We decked the nether heaven with lamps, and rendered it inviolable. That is the measuring of the Mighty, the Knower.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Then He set them up as seven heavens in two days, and revealed [to the angels] in each heaven its ordinance. We have adorned the lowest heaven with lamps, and guarded them. That is the ordaining of the All-mighty, the All-knowing.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: In two days He determined them seven heavens, and He revealed to each heaven its commands. We decorated the lowest heaven with lamps and preserve them. Such is the decree of the Almighty, the Knower.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And He completed them as seven heavens within two days and inspired in each heaven its command. And We adorned the nearest heaven with lamps and as protection. That is the determination of the Exalted in Might, the Knowing.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He formed the seven heavens in two days and revealed to each one its task. He decked the sky above the earth with torches and protected it from (intruders).. Such is the design of the Majestic and All-knowing God".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So He ordained them seven heavens in two periods, and revealed in every heaven its affair; and We adorned the lower heaven with brilliant stars and (made it) to guard; that is the decree of the Mighty, the Knowing.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and in two Days He formed seven heavens, and revealed to each heaven its functions; and We adorned the lower heaven with brilliant lamps [stars] and guarded it. That is the decree of the Almighty, the All Knowing.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: So He completed them as seven firmaments in two Days, and He assigned to each heaven its duty and command. And We adorned the lower heaven with lights, and (provided it) with guard. Such is the Decree of (Him) the Exalted in Might, Full of Knowledge.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faqadahunna sabAAa samawatin fee yawmayni waawha fee kulli samain amraha wazayyanna alssamaa alddunya bimasabeeha wahifthan thalika taqdeeru alAAazeezi alAAaleemi

13.arabic: فَإِنْ أَعْرَضُوا۟ فَقُلْ أَنذَرْتُكُمْ صَـٰعِقَةً مِّثْلَ صَـٰعِقَةِ عَادٍ وَثَمُودَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: If even then they turn away, tell them: "I forewarn you of a terrible punishment like the thunderbolt that fell upon the 'Ad and Thamud."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Then if they turn away say, “I warn you of a thunderbolt like the thunderbolt which came upon A’ad and Thamud.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And We adorned the lower heaven with lamps, and to reserve; that is the ordaining of the All-mighty, the All-knowing. But if they turn away, then say, 'I warn you of a thunderbolt like to the thunderbolt of Ad and Thamood.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Then if they Still turn away, say thou; I warn you of a calamity like the calamity of the 'Aad and Thamud.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: But if they turn away, then say (O Muhammad SAW): "I have warned you of a Sa'iqah (a destructive awful cry, torment, hit, a thunderbolt) like the Sa'iqah which overtook 'Ad and Thamud (people)."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: But if they turn away, say, “I have warned you of a thunderbolt, like the thunderbolt of Aad and Thamood.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: But if they turn away, tell them: “I warn you against a sudden scourge like that which struck Ad and Thamud.”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: But if they turn away, then say: "I have warned you of a Sa`iqah like the Sa`iqah which overtook `Ad and Thamud."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: But if they turn away, then say: I warn you of a thunderbolt like the thunderbolt (which fell of old upon the tribes) of A'ad and Thamud;

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: But if they turn away, say, ‘I warn you of a thunderbolt, like the thunderbolt of ‘Ad and Thamud.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: But if they turn away, say: 'I have given you warning of a thunderbolt similar to that which overtook Aad and Thamood'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But if they turn away, then say, "I have warned you of a thunderbolt like the thunderbolt [that struck] 'Aad and Thamud.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: If they ignore (your message), tell them, "I have warned you against a destructive blast of sound like that which struck the people of `Ad and Thamud.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: But if they turn aside, then say: I have warned you of a scourge like the scourge of Ad and Samood.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: If they turn away, then say, "I warn you of a lightning-bolt like the one which struck the 'Ad and the Thamud:

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But if they turn away, say thou: "I have warned you of a stunning Punishment (as of thunder and lightning) like that which (overtook) the 'Ad and the Thamud!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fain aAAradoo fuqul anthartukum saAAiqatan mithla saAAiqati AAadin wathamooda

14.arabic: إِذْ جَآءَتْهُمُ ٱلرُّسُلُ مِنۢ بَيْنِ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِمْ أَلَّا تَعْبُدُوٓا۟ إِلَّا ٱللَّهَ ۖ قَالُوا۟ لَوْ شَآءَ رَبُّنَا لَأَنزَلَ مَلَـٰٓئِكَةً فَإِنَّا بِمَآ أُرْسِلْتُم بِهِۦ كَـٰفِرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Their apostles came to them one after the other (saying): "Do not worship any one but God." They said: "If our Lord had pleased He would have sent the angels down. We reject what is sent with you."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: When their Noble Messengers approached them from front and from behind saying, "Do not worship any one except Allah"; they said, "If our Lord willed, He would surely have sent down angels – we therefore deny whatever you have been sent with.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: When the Messengers came unto them from before them and from behind them, saying, 'Serve none but God,' they said, 'Had our Lord willed, surely He would have sent down angels; so we disbelieve in the Message you were sent with.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Recall what time the apostles came Unto them from before them and behind them, saying: worship none save Allah. They said: had our Lord listed, He surely would have sent down angels, so verily in that wherewith ye have been sent we are disbelievers altogether.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: When the Messengers came to them, from before them and behind them (saying): "Worship none but Allah" They said: "If our Lord had so willed, He would surely have sent down the angels. So indeed! We disbelieve in that with which you have been sent."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Their messengers came to them, from before them and from behind them, saying, “Do not worship anyone but God.” They said, “Had our Lord willed, He would have sent down angels; Therefore, we reject what you are sent with.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: When the Messengers (of Allah) came to them from the front and from the rear, saying: “Do not serve any but Allah”; they said: “Had our Lord so willed, He would have sent down angels. So we deny the Message you have brought.”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: When the Messengers came to them, from before them and behind them (saying): "Worship none but Allah," they said: "If our Lord had so willed, He would surely have sent down the angels. So, indeed we disbelieve in that with which you have been sent."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: When their messengers came unto them from before them and behind them, saying: Worship none but Allah! they said: If our Lord had willed, He surely would have sent down angels (unto us), so lo! we are disbelievers in that wherewith ye have been sent.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When the apostles came to them, before them and in their own time, saying, ‘Worship no one except Allah!’ They said, ‘Had our Lord wished, He would have sent down angels [to us]. We indeed disbelieve in what you have been sent with.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: When their Messengers came to them from before them and behind them, (saying): 'Worship none except Allah' they answered: 'Had it been the will of our Lord, He would have sent down angels. So we disbelieve in the Message with which you were sent'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [That occurred] when the messengers had come to them before them and after them, [saying], "Worship not except Allah." They said, "If our Lord had willed, He would have sent down the angels, so indeed we, in that with which you have been sent, are disbelievers."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When Messengers from all sides came to them saying, "Do not worship anything besides God, " they said, "Had our Lord wanted, He would have sent us angelic Messengers. We do not believe in your message".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: When their apostles came to them from before them and from behind them, saying, Serve nothing but Allah, they said: If our Lord had pleased He would certainly have sent down angels, so we are surely unbelievers in that with which you are sent.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: when the messengers came to them from before them and behind them, saying, 'Worship none but God!" They said, "If our Lord had willed, He would have sent down angels [to us]. Therefore, we shall never believe in your message.'"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Behold, the messengers came to them, from before them and behind them, (preaching): "Serve none but Allah." They said, "If our Lord had so pleased, He would certainly have sent down angels (to preach). Now we reject your mission (altogether)."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ith jaathumu alrrusulu min bayni aydeehim wamin khalfihim alla taAAbudoo illa Allaha qaloo law shaa rabbuna laanzala malaikatan fainna bima orsiltum bihi kafiroona

15.arabic: فَأَمَّا عَادٌ فَٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ بِغَيْرِ ٱلْحَقِّ وَقَالُوا۟ مَنْ أَشَدُّ مِنَّا قُوَّةً ۖ أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَهُمْ هُوَ أَشَدُّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةً ۖ وَكَانُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا يَجْحَدُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So those who were 'Ad turned insolent unjustly in the land, and said: "Who is stronger than us?" Did they not see that God who created them was greater far in power than they? Yet they refused to believe Our signs.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So regarding the A’ad, they were haughty in the land without right, and they said, “Who is more powerful than us?” Did they not realise that Allah, Who created them, is more powerful than them? And they used to deny Our signs.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: As for Ad, they waxed proud in the earth without right, and they said, 'Who is stronger than we in might?' What, did they not see that God, who created them, was stronger than they in might? And they denied Our signs.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: As for the tribe of 'Aad - they grew stiff-necked on the earth without justification, and said: who is mightier than we in strength? Saw they not that Allah who created them,-He was Mightier than they in strength? And Our signs they were wont to gainsay.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: As for 'Ad, they were arrogant in the land without right, and they said: "Who is mightier than us in strength?" See they not that Allah, Who created them was mightier in strength than them. And they used to deny Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, revelations, etc.)!

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: As for Aad, they turned arrogant on earth, and opposed justice, and said, “Who is more powerful than us?” Have they not considered that God, who created them, is more powerful than they? And they went on denying Our revelations.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: As for Ad, they waxed proud in the land without justification and said: “Who is greater than we in strength?” Did they not see that Allah, Who created them, is greater in strength than they? They continued to deny Our Signs,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: As for `Ad, they were arrogant in the land without right, and they said: "Who is mightier than us in strength" See they not that Allah Who created them was mightier in strength than them. And they used to deny Our Ayat!

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: As for A'ad, they were arrogant in the land without right, and they said: Who is mightier than us in power? Could they not see that Allah Who created them, He was mightier than them in power? And they denied Our revelations.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: As for [the people of] ‘Ad, they acted arrogantly in the earth unduly, and they said, ‘Who is more powerful than us?’ Did they not see that Allah, who created them, is more powerful than them? They used to impugn Our signs;

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: As for Aad, they behaved proudly in the earth without right. 'Who is stronger than us' they would say. Could they not see that Allah, who created them, is stronger than they? But they disbelieved Our signs.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: As for 'Aad, they were arrogant upon the earth without right and said, "Who is greater than us in strength?" Did they not consider that Allah who created them was greater than them in strength? But they were rejecting Our signs.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The people of Ad unjustly sought dominance on earth saying, "Who is more powerful than us?" Did they not consider that God created them and that He is more powerful than they were?" They rejected Our revelations.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then as to Ad, they were unjustly proud in the land, and they said: Who is mightier in strength than we? Did they not see that Allah Who created them was mightier than they in strength, and they denied Our communications?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: As for the tribe of 'Ad, they behaved arrogantly in the land without any justification and said, "Who is mightier than we in power?" Did they not see that God, who created them, was mightier than they in power? Still they continued to deny Our signs,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Now the 'Ad behaved arrogantly through the land, against (all) truth and reason, and said: "Who is superior to us in strength?" What! did they not see that Allah, Who created them, was superior to them in strength? But they continued to reject Our Signs!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faamma AAadun faistakbaroo fee alardi bighayri alhaqqi waqaloo man ashaddu minna quwwatan awalam yaraw anna Allaha allathee khalaqahum huwa ashaddu minhum quwwatan wakanoo biayatina yajhadoona

16.arabic: فَأَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ رِيحًا صَرْصَرًا فِىٓ أَيَّامٍ نَّحِسَاتٍ لِّنُذِيقَهُمْ عَذَابَ ٱلْخِزْىِ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۖ وَلَعَذَابُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ أَخْزَىٰ ۖ وَهُمْ لَا يُنصَرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So We let loose on them a violent wind for several days of distress to make them taste a most disgraceful punishment here in this world, and far more shameful will be the punishment in the Hereafter, and there will be no succour for them.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: We therefore sent a violent thunderstorm towards them in their days of misfortune, in order to make them taste a disgraceful punishment in the life of this world; and indeed the punishment of the Hereafter is more disgracing, and they will not be helped.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Then We loosed against them a wind clamorous in days of ill fortune, that We might let them taste the chastisement of degradation in the present life; and the chastisement of the world to come is even more degrading, and they shall not be helped.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Wherefore We sent upon them a raging wind in days inauspicious, that We might make them taste the torment of humiliation in the life of the world; and surely the torment of the Hereafter will be more humiliating, nor will they be succoured.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So We sent upon them furious wind in days of evil omen (for them) that We might give them a taste of disgracing torment in this present worldly life, but surely the torment of the Hereafter will be more disgracing, and they will never be helped.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So We unleashed upon them a screaming wind, for a few miserable days, to make them taste the punishment of shame in this life; but the punishment of the Hereafter is more shameful; and they will not be saved.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: whereupon We sent upon them a fierce wind on inauspicious days that We might make them taste a degrading chastisement in the life of this world. And surely the chastisement of the Hereafter is even more degrading. There will be none to help them there.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: So, We sent upon them a Sarsar wind in days of calamity that We might give them a taste of disgracing torment in this present worldly life. But surely the torment of the Hereafter will be more disgracing, and they will never be helped.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Therefor We let loose on them a raging wind in evil days, that We might make them taste the torment of disgrace in the life of the world. And verily the doom of the Hereafter will be more shameful, and they will not be helped.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: so We unleashed upon them an icy gale during ill-fated days, in order that We might make them taste a humiliating punishment in the life of the world. Yet the punishment of the Hereafter will be surely more disgraceful, and they will not be helped.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Then, on the ominous days, We loosed against them a howling wind that We might let them taste the punishment of humiliation in this life; but more humiliating will be the punishment of the Everlasting Life and they will not be helped.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So We sent upon them a screaming wind during days of misfortune to make them taste the punishment of disgrace in the worldly life; but the punishment of the Hereafter is more disgracing, and they will not be helped.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We sent upon them a violent wind during a few ill-fated days to make them suffer a disgraceful torment in this life. Their torment in the life to come will be even more disgraceful and they will not receive any help.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So We sent on them a furious wind in unlucky days, that We may make them taste the chastisement of abasement in this world's life; and certainly the chastisement of the hereafter is much more abasing, and they shall not be helped.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: so We let loose upon them a raging wind over several inauspicious days, so that We might make them taste the torment of humiliation in the life of this world, and surely the torment of the Hereafter will be more humiliating. They shall have none to help them.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: So We sent against them a furious Wind through days of disaster, that We might give them a taste of a Penalty of humiliation in this life; but the Penalty of a Hereafter will be more humiliating still: and they will find no help.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faarsalna AAalayhim reehan sarsaran fee ayyamin nahisatin linutheeqahum AAathaba alkhizyi fee alhayati alddunya walaAAathabu alakhirati akhza wahum la yunsaroona

17.arabic: وَأَمَّا ثَمُودُ فَهَدَيْنَـٰهُمْ فَٱسْتَحَبُّوا۟ ٱلْعَمَىٰ عَلَى ٱلْهُدَىٰ فَأَخَذَتْهُمْ صَـٰعِقَةُ ٱلْعَذَابِ ٱلْهُونِ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَكْسِبُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: As for the (tribe of) Thamud, We tried to guide them, but they preferred blindness to guidance; then they were seized by the torment of a humiliating punishment as requital for their misdeeds;

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And regarding the Thamud, We showed them the right path – so they chose to be blind above being guided, therefore the thunderbolt of the disgraceful punishment overcame them – the recompense of their deeds.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: As for Thamood, We guided them, but they preferred blindness above guidance, so the thunderbolt of the chastisement of humiliation seized them for that they were earning.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And as for the tribe of the Thamud We guided them but they preferred blindness to the guidance, wherefore the bolt of the torment of abjection laid hold of them because of that which they had been earning.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And as for Thamud, We showed and made clear to them the Path of Truth (Islamic Monotheism) through Our Messenger, (i.e. showed them the way of success), but they preferred blindness to guidance, so the Sa'iqah (a destructive awful cry, torment, hit, a thunderbolt) of disgracing torment seized them, because of what they used to earn.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And as for Thamood, We guided them, but they preferred blindness over guidance. So the thunderbolt of the humiliating punishment seized them, because of what they used to earn.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: As for Thamud, We bestowed guidance upon them, but they preferred to remain blind rather than be guided. At last a humiliating scourge overtook them on account of their misdeeds.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And as for Thamud, We showed them the path of truth but they preferred blindness to guidance; so the Sa`iqah of disgracing torment seized them because of what they used to earn.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And as for Thamud, We gave them guidance, but they preferred blindness to the guidance, so the bolt of the doom of humiliation overtook them because of what they used to earn.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: As for [the people of] Thamud, We guided them, but they preferred blindness to guidance. So the bolt of a humiliating punishment seized them because of what they used to earn.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: As for Thamood, We (offered) them Our Guidance, but they preferred blindness to guidance. So a thunderbolt of the humiliating punishment seized them because of what they had earned;

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And as for Thamud, We guided them, but they preferred blindness over guidance, so the thunderbolt of humiliating punishment seized them for what they used to earn.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We sent guidance to the people of Thamud but they preferred blindness to guidance so a humiliating blast of torment struck them for their evil deeds.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And as to Samood, We showed them the right way, but they chose error above guidance, so there overtook them the scourge of an abasing chastisement for what they earned.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: As for the Thamud We offered them Our guidance, but they preferred blindness to guidance. So the lightning-bolt of the punishment of humiliation seized them on account of their misdeeds.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: As to the Thamud, We gave them Guidance, but they preferred blindness (of heart) to Guidance: so the stunning Punishment of humiliation seized them, because of what they had earned.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waamma thamoodu fahadaynahum faistahabboo alAAama AAala alhuda faakhathathum saAAiqatu alAAathabi alhooni bima kanoo yaksiboona

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But We saved those who believed and took heed for themselves.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And We rescued those who accepted faith and were pious.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And We delivered those who believed and were godfearing.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And We delivered those who believed and had been fearing.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And We saved those who believed and used to fear Allah, keep their duty to Him and avoid evil.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And We saved those who believed and were righteous.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Yet We delivered those who believed and were God-fearing.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And We saved those who believed and had Taqwa.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And We delivered those who believed and used to keep their duty to Allah.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And We delivered those who had faith and were Godwary.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: and We saved those who believed and feared Allah.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And We saved those who believed and used to fear Allah.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We only rescued the believers who had fear of God.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And We delivered those who believed and guarded (against evil).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We saved those who had attained to faith and were God-fearing.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But We delivered those who believed and practised righteousness.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wanajjayna allatheena amanoo wakanoo yattaqoona

19.arabic: وَيَوْمَ يُحْشَرُ أَعْدَآءُ ٱللَّهِ إِلَى ٱلنَّارِ فَهُمْ يُوزَعُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The day the enemies of God will be gathered at the Fire and the records of their deeds will be distributed,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And on the day when the enemies of Allah are herded towards the fire, the earlier groups will be restrained till the latter join them.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Upon the day when God's enemies are mustered to the Fire, duly disposed,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And warn them of the Day whereon the enemies of Allah will be gathered Unto the Fire; and they will be set in bands.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And (remember) the Day that the enemies of Allah will be gathered to the Fire, so they will be collected there (the first and the last).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The Day when God’s enemies are herded into the Fire, forcibly.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Imagine the Day when Allah's enemies will be mustered to the Fire, and the people of the former times will be detained until the arrival of people of the later times,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And (remember) the Day that the enemies of Allah will be gathered to the Fire, then they will be driven.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And (make mention of) the day when the enemies of Allah are gathered unto the Fire, they are driven on

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: The day the enemies of Allah are marched out toward the Fire, while they are in check,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: On the Day when the enemies of Allah will be rightfully gathered together before Fire,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And [mention, O Muhammad], the Day when the enemies of Allah will be gathered to the Fire while they are [driven] assembled in rows,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They will be spurred on

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And on the day that the enemies of Allah shall be brought together to the fire, then they shall be formed into groups.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: On the Day that the enemies of God shall be gathered together and driven to the Fire, they shall be formed into groups, and

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: On the Day that the enemies of Allah will be gathered together to the Fire, they will be marched in ranks.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wayawma yuhsharu aAAdao Allahi ila alnnari fahum yoozaAAoona

20.arabic: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا مَا جَآءُوهَا شَهِدَ عَلَيْهِمْ سَمْعُهُمْ وَأَبْصَـٰرُهُمْ وَجُلُودُهُم بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So that when they reach it their ears and eyes and persons will testify to what they did.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: To the extent that when they reach it, their ears and their eyes and their skins will testify against them for what they used to do.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: till when they are come to it, their hearing, their eyes and their skins bear witness against them concerning what they have been doing,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Until when they come to it, their ears and their sights and their skins will bear witness against them of that which they had been working.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Till, when they reach it (Hell-fire), their hearing (ears) and their eyes, and their skins will testify against them as to what they used to do.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Until, when they have reached it, their hearing, and their sight, and their skins will testify against them regarding what they used to do.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and when all have arrived, their ears, their eyes, and their skins shall bear witness against them, stating all that they had done in the life of the world.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Till, when they reach it, their hearing (ears) and their eyes and their skins will testify against them as to what they used to do.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Till, when they reach it, their ears and their eyes and their skins testify against them as to what they used to do.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: when they come to it, their hearing, their eyes and their skins will bear witness against them concerning what they used to do.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: when they reach it, their hearing, eyes and skins will testify against them for what they were doing.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Until, when they reach it, their hearing and their eyes and their skins will testify against them of what they used to do.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: until (on the brink of it) their eyes, ears and skin will testify to their deeds on the Day when the enemies of God are driven to the fire.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Until when they come to it, their ears and their eyes and their skins shall bear witness against them as to what they did.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: when they come close to it, their ears, eyes and skins will testify against them for their misdeeds.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: At length, when they reach the (Fire), their hearing, their sight, and their skins will bear witness against them, as to (all) their deeds.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Hatta itha ma jaooha shahida AAalayhim samAAuhum waabsaruhum wajulooduhum bima kanoo yaAAmaloona

21.arabic: وَقَالُوا۟ لِجُلُودِهِمْ لِمَ شَهِدتُّمْ عَلَيْنَا ۖ قَالُوٓا۟ أَنطَقَنَا ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنطَقَ كُلَّ شَىْءٍ وَهُوَ خَلَقَكُمْ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And they will say to their bodies: "Why did you testify against us?" They will answer: "God, who gave all things power of articulation, made us speak. It is He who created you the first time, and to Him you will return.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And they will say to their skins, “Why did you testify against us?"; they will say, “Allah has made us talk, Who has given all things the power of speech, and it is He Who created you the first time, and it is to Him that you have to return.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and they will say to their skins, 'Why bore you witness against us?' They shall say, 'God gave us speech, as He gave everything speech. He created you the first time, and unto Him you shall be returned.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And they will say Unto their skins: wherefore bear ye witness against us They will say: Allah hath caused us to speak, Who causeth everything to speak, and Who created you the first time and Unto whom ye are now caused to return.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And they will say to their skins, "Why do you testify against us?" They will say: "Allah has caused us to speak, as He causes all things to speak, and He created you the first time, and to Him you are made to return."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And they will say to their skins, “Why did you testify against us?” They will say, “God, Who made all things speak, made us speak. It is He who created you the first time, and to Him you are returned.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They will ask their skins: “Why did you bear witness against us?” The skins will reply: “Allah gave us speech, as He gave speech to all others. He it is Who created you for the first time and it is to Him that you will be sent back.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And they will say to their skins, "Why do you testify against us" They will say: "Allah has caused us to speak - as He causes all things to speak, and He created you the first time, and to Him you are made to return."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And they say unto their skins: Why testify ye against us? They say: Allah hath given us speech Who giveth speech to all things, and Who created you at the first, and unto Whom ye are returned.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They will say to their skins, ‘Why did you bear witness against us?’ They will say, ‘We were given speech by Allah, who gave speech to all things. He created you the first time, and you are being brought back to Him.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: 'Why did you bear witness against us' they will ask their skins, and they will reply: 'Allah has given us speech, as He has given speech to everything. It was He who created you the first time, and to Him you shall return.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And they will say to their skins, "Why have you testified against us?" They will say, "We were made to speak by Allah, who has made everything speak; and He created you the first time, and to Him you are returned.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They will ask their own skin, "Why did you testify against us?" They will reply, "God, who has made everything speak, made us also speak. It was He Who created you in the first place and to Him you have returned.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And they shall say to their skins: Why have you borne witness against us? They shall say: Allah Who makes everything speak has made us speak, and He created you at first, and to Him you shall be brought back.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: And they will ask their skins, "Why did you bear witness against us?" and their skins will reply, "God, who gives speech to all things, has given speech to us [as well], it was He who created you in the first instance and to Him you are [now] brought back,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They will say to their skins: "Why bear ye witness against us?" They will say: "Allah hath given us speech,- (He) Who giveth speech to everything: He created you for the first time, and unto Him were ye to return.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waqaloo lijuloodihim lima shahidtum AAalayna qaloo antaqana Allahu allathee antaqa kulla shayin wahuwa khalaqakum awwala marratin wailayhi turjaAAoona

22.arabic: وَمَا كُنتُمْ تَسْتَتِرُونَ أَن يَشْهَدَ عَلَيْكُمْ سَمْعُكُمْ وَلَآ أَبْصَـٰرُكُمْ وَلَا جُلُودُكُمْ وَلَـٰكِن ظَنَنتُمْ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَعْلَمُ كَثِيرًا مِّمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: You did not hide your (doings) so that your ears or eyes or persons should not testify against you. In fact you thought that God did not know the things you used to do.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And where could you hide from Him, so that your ears and your eyes and your skins may not testify against you? But you had assumed that Allah does not know most of your deeds!

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Not so did you cover yourselves, that your hearing, your eyes and your skins should not bear witness against you; but you thought that God would never know much of the things that you were working.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Ye have not been taking cover against yourselves lest your ears and your eyes and your skins should bear witness against you, but ye imagined that Allah not much knew of that which ye were working.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And you have not been hiding against yourselves, lest your ears, and your eyes, and your skins testify against you, but you thought that Allah knew not much of what you were doing.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: You were unable to hide yourselves from your hearing, and your sight, and your skins, to prevent them from testifying against you, and you imagined that God was unaware of much of what you do.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: When you used to conceal yourselves (while committing misdeeds) you never thought that your ears or your eyes or your skins would ever bear witness against you; you rather fancied that Allah does not know a great deal of what you do.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And you have not been hiding yourselves (in the world), lest your ears and your eyes and your skins should testify against you; but you thought that Allah knew not much of what you were doing.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Ye did not hide yourselves lest your ears and your eyes and your skins should testify against you, but ye deemed that Allah knew not much of what ye did.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: You did not use to conceal yourselves [while perpetrating sinful acts] lest your hearing, your eyes, or your skins should bear witness against you, but you thought that Allah did not know most of what you did.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: It is not that you covered yourselves so that your hearing, eyes and skin could not bear witness against you but you thought that Allah did not know much of that which you do.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And you were not covering yourselves, lest your hearing testify against you or your sight or your skins, but you assumed that Allah does not know much of what you do.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: You did not (think) to hide your deeds from your ears, eyes and skin and you felt that God would not know all that you had been doing.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And you did not veil yourselves lest your ears and your eyes and your skins should bear witness against you, but you thought that Allah did not know most of what you did.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: you could not hide yourselves from your ears and your eyes and your skins to prevent them from testifying against you, and you thought that God would never know much of what you did,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "Ye did not seek to hide yourselves, lest your hearing, your sight, and your skins should bear witness against you! But ye did think that Allah knew not many of the things that ye used to do!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wama kuntum tastatiroona an yashhada AAalaykum samAAukum wala absarukum wala juloodukum walakin thanantum anna Allaha la yaAAlamu katheeran mimma taAAmaloona

23.arabic: وَذَٰلِكُمْ ظَنُّكُمُ ٱلَّذِى ظَنَنتُم بِرَبِّكُمْ أَرْدَىٰكُمْ فَأَصْبَحْتُم مِّنَ ٱلْخَـٰسِرِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: It is this notion you had of your Lord that caused your ruin, and you are lost."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And this is the assumption you had regarding your Lord, which has ruined you, so you are now among those who have been defeated.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: That then, the thought you thought about your Lord, has destroyed you, and therefore you find yourselves this morning among the losers.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: That thought of yours which ye formed concerning Your Lord, hath ruined you, and ye have become of the losers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And that thought of yours which you thought about your Lord, has brought you to destruction, and you have become (this Day) of those utterly lost!

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: It is that thought of yours about your Lord that led you to ruin—so you became of the losers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: This thought of yours about your Lord has led to your perdition and you have become among the losers.”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And that thought of yours which you thought about your Lord, has brought you to destruction; and you have become of those utterly lost!

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: That, your thought which ye did think about your Lord, hath ruined you; and ye find yourselves (this day) among the lost.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: That misjudgement, which you entertained about your Lord ruined you. So you became losers.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Rather, it is the thoughts you thought about your Lord that have destroyed you, therefore, this morning you find yourselves among the losers'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And that was your assumption which you assumed about your Lord. It has brought you to ruin, and you have become among the losers."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: This was how you considered your Lord, but He knows you better than you know yourselves. Thus, you are now lost".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And that was your (evil) thought which you entertained about your Lord that has tumbled you down into perdition, so are you become of the lost ones.
