translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: but these thoughts which you entertained concerning your Lord, have brought you to destruction, and [now] you are among the utterly lost!"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "But this thought of yours which ye did entertain concerning your Lord, hath brought you to destruction, and (now) have ye become of those utterly lost!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wathalikum thannukumu allathee thanantum birabbikum ardakum faasbahtum mina alkhasireena

24.arabic: فَإِن يَصْبِرُوا۟ فَٱلنَّارُ مَثْوًى لَّهُمْ ۖ وَإِن يَسْتَعْتِبُوا۟ فَمَا هُم مِّنَ ٱلْمُعْتَبِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Even if they are patient, their abode is Hell; and if they beg for favour, none will favour them.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Then if they are patient, even then the fire is their home; and if they plead, none will listen to their pleading.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Then if they persist, the Fire shall be a lodging for them; and if they ask amends yet no amends shall be made to them.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Then if they are patient, the Fire is their home, and if they seek to please Allah, they will not be of those who are allowed to please Allah.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then, if they have patience, yet the Fire will be a home for them, and if they beg for to be excused, yet they are not of those who will ever be excused.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: If they endure patiently, the Fire will be their residence; and if they make up excuses, they will not be pardoned.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: In this state, whether they bear with patience (or not), Fire alone shall be their abode. And if they seek to make amends, they will not be allowed to do so.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then, if they bear the torment patiently, then the Fire is the home for them, and if they seek to please Allah, yet they are not of those who will ever be allowed to please Allah.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And though they are resigned, yet the Fire is still their home; and if they ask for favour, yet they are not of those unto whom favour can be shown.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Should they be patient, the Fire is their abode; and should they seek to propitiate [Allah], they will not be redeemed.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Even if they are patient the Fire shall still be their lodging, and if they seek pardon, they shall not be among those who are pardoned.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So [even] if they are patient, the Fire is a residence for them; and if they ask to appease [Allah], they will not be of those who are allowed to appease.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Even if they were to exercise patience, their dwelling would still be hell fire. Even if they were to seek favors, they would receive none.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then if they will endure, still the fire is their abode, and if they ask for goodwill, then are they not of those who shall be granted goodwill.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Even if they are patient, the Fire will still be their homes. And if they pray to be allowed to make amends, they will not be allowed to do so.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: If, then, they have patience, the Fire will be a home for them! and if they beg to be received into favour, into favour will they not (then) be received.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fain yasbiroo faalnnaru mathwan lahum wain yastaAAtiboo fama hum mina almuAAtabeena

25.arabic: وَقَيَّضْنَا لَهُمْ قُرَنَآءَ فَزَيَّنُوا۟ لَهُم مَّا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ وَحَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْقَوْلُ فِىٓ أُمَمٍ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِن قَبْلِهِم مِّنَ ٱلْجِنِّ وَٱلْإِنسِ ۖ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ خَـٰسِرِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We had assigned to them close companions who made their past and present look attractive to them; and the fate that had once befallen the communities of jinns and men before them was justified upon them. They were indeed bound to perish.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And We appointed companions for them, who made what is before them and what is after them seem good to them, and the Word proved true upon them along with the groups of jinns and men who passed away before them; they were indeed losers.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: We have allotted them comrades, and they have decked out fair to them that which is before them and behind them. So against them has been realized the Word concerning nations that passed away before them, men and jinn alike; surely they were losers.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And We have assigned Unto them some companions who made fairseeming Unto them that which was before them and that which was behind them justified upon them was the Word Pronounced on the communities of the jinn and mankind who passed away before them; verily they were the losers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And We have assigned them (devils) intimate companions (in this world), who have made fair-seeming to them, what was before them (evil deeds which they were doing in the present worldly life and disbelief in the Reckoning and the Resurrection, etc.) and what was behind them (denial of the matters in the coming life of the Hereafter as regards punishment or reward, etc.). And the Word (i.e. the torment) is justified against them as it was justified against those who were among the previous generations of jinns and men that had passed away before them. Indeed they (all) were the losers.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: We had assigned companions for them, who glamorized to them what was in front of them, and what was behind them. And the Word proved true against them in communities of jinn and humans that have passed away before them. They were losers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: We had assigned to them companions who embellished for them all that was before them and behind them. Thus the same decree (of chastisement) which had overtaken the previous generations of jinn and human beings (also) became due against them. Surely they became the losers.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And We have assigned for them intimate companions (in this world), who have made fair seeming to them, what was before them and what was behind them. And the Word is justified against them as it was justified against those who were among the previous generations of Jinn and men that had passed away before them. Indeed they (all) were the losers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And We assigned them comrades (in the world), who made their present and their past fairseeming unto them. And the Word concerning nations of the jinn and humankind who passed away before them hath effect for them. Lo! they were ever losers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: We have assigned them companions who make their present [conduct] and future [state] seem decorous to them, and the word became due against them along with the nations of jinn and humans that passed away before them. They were indeed losers.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We have assigned companions to them, who make what is before them and behind them seem fair to them. The statement has been realized against them in nations of people and jinn alike that passed away before them they were indeed the losers.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And We appointed for them companions who made attractive to them what was before them and what was behind them [of sin], and the word has come into effect upon them among nations which had passed on before them of jinn and men. Indeed, they [all] were losers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We assigned for them companions who would make their past and present (deeds) seem attractive to them. Thus, they became subject to what the jinn and human beings before were destined to suffer. They were certainly lost.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And We have appointed for them comrades so they have made fair-seeming to them what is before them and what is behind them, and the word proved true against them-- among the nations of the jinn and the men that have passed away before them-- that they shall surely be losers.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We assigned to them companions who made their doings appear fair to them. But the same decree [of punishment] proved true against them, which had proved true against nations of jinn and mankind who passed away before them. Surely they were the losers.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And We have destined for them intimate companions (of like nature), who made alluring to them what was before them and behind them; and the sentence among the previous generations of Jinns and men, who have passed away, is proved against them; for they are utterly lost.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waqayyadna lahum quranaa fazayyanoo lahum ma bayna aydeehim wama khalfahum wahaqqa AAalayhimu alqawlu fee omamin qad khalat min qablihim mina aljinni waalinsi innahum kanoo khasireena

26.arabic: وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَا تَسْمَعُوا۟ لِهَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانِ وَٱلْغَوْا۟ فِيهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَغْلِبُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The disbelievers say: "Do not listen to this Qur'an, and shout away (its reading); you may haply prevail."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And the disbelievers said, “Do not listen to this Qur’an and engulf it in noise – perhaps you may be victorious this way.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: The unbelievers say, 'Do not give ear to this Koran, and talk idly about it; haply you will overcome.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And those who disbelieve say: hearken not Unto this Qur'an and babble therein, haply ye may overcome.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And those who disbelieve say: "Listen not to this Quran, and make noise in the midst of its (recitation) that you may overcome."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those who disbelieve say, “Do not listen to this Quran, and talk over it, so that you may prevail.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: The deniers of the Truth say: “Do not give ear to the Qur'an and cause interruption when it is recited; thus perhaps you will gain the upper hand.”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And those who disbelieve say: "Listen not to this Qur'an, and make noise in the midst of its (recitation) that you may overcome."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Those who disbelieve say: Heed not this Qur'an, and drown the hearing of it; haply ye may conquer.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: The faithless say, ‘Do not listen to this Quran and hoot it down so that you may prevail [over the Apostle].’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: The unbelievers say: 'Do not listen to this Koran, and talk idly about it so that you might be overcome'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And those who disbelieve say, "Do not listen to this Qur'an and speak noisily during [the recitation of] it that perhaps you will overcome."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The disbelievers say, "Do not listen to this Quran but make a lot of unnecessary noise while it is being read so that perhaps you will defeat it".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And those who disbelieve say: Do not listen to this Quran and make noise therein, perhaps you may overcome.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Those who deny the truth say, "Do not listen to this Quran. Drown it out with noise, so that you may gain the upper hand."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: The Unbelievers say: "Listen not to this Qur'an, but talk at random in the midst of its (reading), that ye may gain the upper hand!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waqala allatheena kafaroo la tasmaAAoo lihatha alqurani wailghaw feehi laAAallakum taghliboona

27.arabic: فَلَنُذِيقَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ عَذَابًا شَدِيدًا وَلَنَجْزِيَنَّهُمْ أَسْوَأَ ٱلَّذِى كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We shall make the disbelievers taste the severest punishment, and retribute them for the worst that they had done.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So We will indeed make the disbelievers taste the severe punishment, and indeed We will repay them for their worst deeds.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So We shall let the unbelievers taste a terrible chastisement, and shall recompense them with the worst of what they were working.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Wherefore We will surely cause those who disbelieve to taste a severe tor ment, and We will surely requite them the worst of that which they have been working.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: But surely, We shall cause those who disbelieve to taste a severe torment, and certainly, We shall requite them the worst of what they used to do.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: We will make those who disbelieve taste an intense agony, and We will recompense them according to the worst of what they used to do.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: We shall certainly make these unbelievers taste a terrible chastisement and shall fully requite them according to the worst deeds that they committed.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: But surely, We shall cause those who disbelieve to taste a severe torment, and certainly, We shall requite them the worst of what they used to do.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: But verily We shall cause those who disbelieve to taste an awful doom, and verily We shall requite them the worst of what they used to do.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: We will surely make the faithless taste a severe punishment, and We will surely requite them by the worst of what they used to do.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We will let the unbelievers taste a terrible punishment, and recompense them with the worst of what they were doing.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But We will surely cause those who disbelieve to taste a severe punishment, and We will surely recompense them for the worst of what they had been doing.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We shall certainly make the unbelievers suffer severe torment and will punish them far worse than what they deserve for their deeds.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Therefore We will most certainly make those who disbelieve taste a severe punishment, and We will most certainly reward them for the evil deeds they used to do.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Therefore, We will most certainly make those who are bent on denying the truth taste a severe punishment and We will most certainly requite them according to the worst of their deeds,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But We will certainly give the Unbelievers a taste of a severe Penalty, and We will requite them for the worst of their deeds.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Falanutheeqanna allatheena kafaroo AAathaban shadeedan walanajziyannahum aswaa allathee kanoo yaAAmaloona

28.arabic: ذَٰلِكَ جَزَآءُ أَعْدَآءِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلنَّارُ ۖ لَهُمْ فِيهَا دَارُ ٱلْخُلْدِ ۖ جَزَآءًۢ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا يَجْحَدُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: This is the requital for God's enemies: Hell, where they will have their lasting home, as punishment for denying Our revelations.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: This is the recompense of Allah’s enemies – the fire; they have to stay in it forever; the penalty for denying Our signs.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: That is the recompense of God's enemies -- the Fire, wherein they shall have the Abode of Eternity as a recompense, for that they denied Our signs.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: That is the meed of the enemies of Allah: the Fire. Therein is their home of Abidence: a meed forasmuch as Our revelations they were wont to gainsay.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: That is the recompense of the enemies of Allah: The Fire, therein will be for them the eternal home, a (deserving) recompense for that they used to deny Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Such is the recompense of God’s enemies—the Fire—where they will have their permanent home, in recompense for having disregarded Our revelations.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: That is the recompense of the enemies of Allah -- the Fire, their abiding home. That will be the re-compense for their denying Our Signs.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: That is the recompense of the enemies of Allah: the Fire. Therein will be for them the eternal home, a recompense for that they used to deny Our Ayat.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: That is the reward of Allah's enemies: the Fire. Therein is their immortal home, payment forasmuch as they denied Our revelations.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: That is the requital of the enemies of Allah—the Fire! In it, they will have an everlasting abode, as a requital for their impugning Our signs.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: The Fire that is the recompense of the enemies of Allah. There it is that they will be lodged for eternity, a recompense for their disbelief of Our verses.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: That is the recompense of the enemies of Allah - the Fire. For them therein is the home of eternity as recompense for what they, of Our verses, were rejecting.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The recompense of the enemies of God for their rejection of Our revelations will be fire as their eternal dwelling.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: That is the reward of the enemies of Allah-- the fire; for them therein shall be the house of long abiding; a reward for their denying Our communications.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: that is the reward of the enemies of God, the Fire will be their everlasting home, a reward for their rejection of Our revelations.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Such is the requital of the enemies of Allah,- the Fire: therein will be for them the Eternal Home: a (fit) requital, for that they were wont to reject Our Signs.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Thalika jazao aAAdai Allahi alnnaru lahum feeha daru alkhuldi jazaan bima kanoo biayatina yajhadoona

29.arabic: وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ رَبَّنَآ أَرِنَا ٱلَّذَيْنِ أَضَلَّانَا مِنَ ٱلْجِنِّ وَٱلْإِنسِ نَجْعَلْهُمَا تَحْتَ أَقْدَامِنَا لِيَكُونَا مِنَ ٱلْأَسْفَلِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Those who disbelieve will say: "O Lord, show us those among the jinns and men who had led us astray that we may trample them underfoot and make them wholly abject."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And the disbelievers said, “Our Lord! Show us both – among jinns and men – who misled us, for us to put them beneath our feet so that they be the lowest of the low.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And the unbelievers shall say, 'Our Lord, show us those that led us astray, both jinn and men, and we shall set them underneath our feet, that they may be among the lower ones.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And those who disbelieve will say: our Lord! show us those, of the jinn and mankind, who led us astray and we will place them underneath our feet that they may be of the nithermost.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And those who disbelieve will say: "Our Lord! Show us those among jinns and men who led us astray, we shall crush them under our feet, so that they become the lowest."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those who disbelieved will say, “Our Lord, show us those who led us astray—among jinn and humans—and we will trample them under our feet, so they become among of the lowest.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: There the unbelievers will say: “Our Lord, show us those that led us astray, both jinn and humans, and we will trample them under our feet so that they are utterly degraded.”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And those who disbelieve will say: "Our Lord! Show us those among Jinn and men who led us astray, that we may crush them under our feet so that they become the lowest."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And those who disbelieve will say: Our Lord! Show us those who beguiled us of the jinn and humankind. We will place them underneath our feet that they may be among the nethermost.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: The faithless will say, ‘Our Lord! Show us those who led us astray from among jinn and humans so that we may trample them under our feet, so that they may be among the lowermost!’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: The unbelievers will say: 'Lord, show us the jinn and people who led us astray, to put them under our feet so that they are among the lowest'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And those who disbelieved will [then] say, "Our Lord, show us those who misled us of the jinn and men [so] we may put them under our feet that they will be among the lowest."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The disbelievers will say, "Lord, show us the human beings and jinn who caused us to go astray. We shall put them under our feet to lower them".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And those who disbelieve will say: Our Lord! show us those who led us astray from among the jinn and the men that we may trample them under our feet so that they may be of the lowest.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Those  who deny the truth will say, "Our Lord, show us those jinn and men who misled us and we shall trample them underfoot, so that they may be among the lowest of the low."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And the Unbelievers will say: "Our Lord! Show us those, among Jinns and men, who misled us: We shall crush them beneath our feet, so that they become the vilest (before all)."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waqala allatheena kafaroo rabbana arina allathayni adallana mina aljinni waalinsi najAAalhuma tahta aqdamina liyakoona mina alasfaleena

30.arabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ قَالُوا۟ رَبُّنَا ٱللَّهُ ثُمَّ ٱسْتَقَـٰمُوا۟ تَتَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ أَلَّا تَخَافُوا۟ وَلَا تَحْزَنُوا۟ وَأَبْشِرُوا۟ بِٱلْجَنَّةِ ٱلَّتِى كُنتُمْ تُوعَدُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Surely the angels will come down to those who say, 'Our Lord is God' and then remain steadfast, saying: "You should have neither fear nor regret, but rejoice in the happy news of Paradise that has been promised you.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Indeed those who said, “Allah is our Lord” and remained firm upon it – upon them descend the angels, (saying), “Do not fear nor grieve, and be happy for the Paradise which you are promised.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Those who have said, 'Our Lord is God.' then have gone straight, upon them the angels descend, saying, 'Fear not, neither sorrow; rejoice in Paradise that you were promised.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily those who say: our Lord is Allah, and have thereafter stood fast by it,- upon them there shall come down the angels: fear not, nor grieve, and rejoice in the tidings of the Garden which ye have been promised.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily, those who say: "Our Lord is Allah (Alone)," and then they Istaqamu, on them the angels will descend (at the time of their death) (saying): "Fear not, nor grieve! But receive the glad tidings of Paradise which you have been promised!

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Surely, those who say: “Our Lord is God,” and then go straight, the angels will descend upon them: “Do not fear, and do not grieve, but rejoice in the news of the Garden which you were promised.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Those who say “Allah is our Lord” and then remain steadfast, upon them descend angels (and say): “Do not fear nor grieve, and receive good tidings of Paradise which you were promised.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, those who say: "Our Lord is Allah," and then they stand firm, on them the angels will descend (saying): "Fear not, nor grieve! But receive the glad tidings of Paradise which you have been promised!"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! those who say: Our Lord is Allah, and afterward are upright, the angels descend upon them, saying: Fear not nor grieve, but hear good tidings of the paradise which ye are promised.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed those who say, ‘Our Lord is Allah!’ and then remain steadfast, the angels descend upon them, [saying,] ‘Do not fear, nor be grieved! Receive the good news of the paradise, which you have been promised.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: The angels will descend on those who said: 'Allah is Our Lord' and have then gone straight, (saying:) 'Be neither fearful, nor sad; rejoice in the Paradise you have been promised.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, those who have said, "Our Lord is Allah " and then remained on a right course - the angels will descend upon them, [saying], "Do not fear and do not grieve but receive good tidings of Paradise, which you were promised.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: To those who have said, "God is our Lord, " and who have remained steadfast to their belief, the angels will descend saying, "Do not be afraid or grieved. Receive the glad news of the Paradise which was promised to you.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: (As for) those who say: Our Lord is Allah, then continue in the right way, the angels descend upon them, saying: Fear not, nor be grieved, and receive good news of the garden which you were promised.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: As for those who affirm, "Our Lord is God," and then remain steadfast, the angels will descend on them, saying, "Have no fear and do not grieve. Rejoice in the [good news of the] Garden that you have been promised.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: In the case of those who say, "Our Lord is Allah", and, further, stand straight and steadfast, the angels descend on them (from time to time): "Fear ye not!" (they suggest), "Nor grieve! but receive the Glad Tidings of the Garden (of Bliss), the which ye were promised!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna allatheena qaloo rabbuna Allahu thumma istaqamoo tatanazzalu AAalayhimu almalaikatu alla takhafoo wala tahzanoo waabshiroo bialjannati allatee kuntum tooAAadoona

31.arabic: نَحْنُ أَوْلِيَآؤُكُمْ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَفِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۖ وَلَكُمْ فِيهَا مَا تَشْتَهِىٓ أَنفُسُكُمْ وَلَكُمْ فِيهَا مَا تَدَّعُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We are your friends in this life and in the Hereafter where you will get whatever your hearts desire, and have whatsoever you ask for,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “We are your friends in the life of this world and in the Hereafter; in it for you is all that you may wish for, and for you is all what you ask.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: We are your friends in the present life and in the world to come; therein you shall have all that your souls desire, all that you call for,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: We are your friends in the life of the world and in the Hereafter; there in yours will be whatsoever your souls desire, and therein yours shall be whatsoever ye call for.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "We have been your friends in the life of this world and are (so) in the Hereafter. Therein you shall have (all) that your inner-selves desire, and therein you shall have (all) for which you ask for.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: We are your allies in this life and in the Hereafter, wherein you will have whatever your souls desire, and you will have therein whatever you call for.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: We are your companions in this world and in the Hereafter. There you shall have all that you desire and all what you will ask for.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "We have been your friends in the life of this world and are (so) in the Hereafter. Therein you shall have (all) that your souls desire, and therein you shall have (all) for which you ask."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: We are your protecting friends in the life of the world and in the Hereafter. There ye will have (all) that your souls desire, and there ye will have (all) for which ye pray.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: We are your friends in the life of this world and in the Hereafter, and you will have in it whatever your souls desire, and you will have in it whatever you ask for,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We are your guides in this world and in the Everlasting Life. There, you shall have all that your souls desire, and all that you ask for

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: We [angels] were your allies in worldly life and [are so] in the Hereafter. And you will have therein whatever your souls desire, and you will have therein whatever you request [or wish]

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We are your guardians in this world and in the life to come, where you will have whatever you call for,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: We are your guardians in this world's life and in the hereafter, and you shall have therein what your souls desire and you shall have therein what you ask for:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: "We are your companions in this life and in the Hereafter. Therein you shall have all that your souls desire, and therein you shall have all that you ask for

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "We are your protectors in this life and in the Hereafter: therein shall ye have all that your souls shall desire; therein shall ye have all that ye ask for!-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Nahnu awliyaokum fee alhayati alddunya wafee alakhirati walakum feeha ma tashtahee anfusukum walakum feeha ma taddaAAoona

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: As a gift from the forgiving, ever-merciful (God)."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “The hospitality of the Oft Forgiving, Most Merciful.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: as hospitality from One All-forgiving, One All-compassionate.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And entertainment from the Forgiving, the Merciful.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "An entertainment from (Allah), the Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: As Hospitality from an All-Forgiving, Merciful One.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: This is by way of hospitality from Him Who is Most Forgiving, Most Merciful.”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "An entertainment from the Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: A gift of welcome from One Forgiving, Merciful.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: as a hospitality from One all-forgiving, all-merciful.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: as hospitality from One, the Forgiving, the Most Merciful'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: As accommodation from a [Lord who is] Forgiving and Merciful."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: a hospitable welcome from the All-forgiving and All-merciful God".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: A provision from the Forgiving, the Merciful.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: as a rich provision from One who is ever forgiving and most merciful."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "A hospitable gift from one Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Nuzulan min ghafoorin raheemin

33.arabic: وَمَنْ أَحْسَنُ قَوْلًا مِّمَّن دَعَآ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ وَعَمِلَ صَـٰلِحًا وَقَالَ إِنَّنِى مِنَ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Whose word is better than his who calls to God and does the right, and says: "I am of the obedient?"

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And whose speech is better than one who calls towards his Lord and does righteous deeds, and says, “I am a Muslim.”?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And who speaks fairer than he who calls unto God and does righteousness and says, 'Surely I am of them that surrender'?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And who is better in speech than he who summoneth Unto Allah, and worketh righteously, and saith: verily I am one of the Muslims.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And who is better in speech than he who [says: "My Lord is Allah (believes in His Oneness)," and then stands straight (acts upon His Order), and] invites (men) to Allah's (Islamic Monotheism), and does righteous deeds, and says: "I am one of the Muslims."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And who is better in speech than someone who calls to God, and acts with integrity, and says, “I am of those who submit”?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And who is fairer in speech than he who calls to Allah and acts righteously and says: “I am a Muslim”?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And who is better in speech than he who invites to Allah, and does righteous deeds, and says: "I am one of the Muslims."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And who is better in speech than him who prayeth unto his Lord and doeth right, and saith: Lo! I am of those who are muslims (surrender unto Him).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Who has a better call than him who summons to Allah and acts righteously and says, ‘Indeed I am one of the Muslims’?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And, who is better in saying than he who invites to Allah, does what is right, and says: 'Surely, I am of those who surrender'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And who is better in speech than one who invites to Allah and does righteousness and says, "Indeed, I am of the Muslims."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Who speaks better than one who invites human beings to God, acts righteously and says, "I am a Muslim".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And who speaks better than he who calls to Allah while he himself does good, and says: I am surely of those who submit?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Who speaks better than one who calls to God and does good works and says, "I am surely of those who submit"?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Who is better in speech than one who calls (men) to Allah, works righteousness, and says, "I am of those who bow in Islam"?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waman ahsanu qawlan mimman daAAa ila Allahi waAAamila salihan waqala innanee mina almuslimeena

34.arabic: وَلَا تَسْتَوِى ٱلْحَسَنَةُ وَلَا ٱلسَّيِّئَةُ ۚ ٱدْفَعْ بِٱلَّتِى هِىَ أَحْسَنُ فَإِذَا ٱلَّذِى بَيْنَكَ وَبَيْنَهُۥ عَدَٰوَةٌ كَأَنَّهُۥ وَلِىٌّ حَمِيمٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Good and evil are not alike. Repel evil with what is good. Then you will find your erstwhile enemy like a close. affectionate friend.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And the good deed and the evil deed will never be equal; O listener! Repel the evil deed with a good one, thereupon the one between whom and you was enmity, will become like a close friend.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Not equal are the good deed and the evil deed. Repel with that which is fairer and behold, he between whom and thee there is enmity shall be as if he were a loyal friend.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Nor can good and evil be equal. Repel thou evil with that which is goodly then behold he between whom and thee there was enmity, will be as though he was a warm friend.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: The good deed and the evil deed cannot be equal. Repel (the evil) with one which is better (i.e. Allah ordered the faithful believers to be patient at the time of anger, and to excuse those who treat them badly), then verily! he, between whom and you there was enmity, (will become) as though he was a close friend.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Good and evil are not equal. Repel evil with good, and the person who was your enemy becomes like an intimate friend.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (O Prophet), good and evil are not equal. Repel (evil) with that which is good, and you will see that he, between whom and you there was enmity, shall become as if he were a bosom friend (of yours).

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: The good deed and the evil deed cannot be equal. Repel (the evil) with one which is better, then verily he, between whom and you there was enmity, (will become) as though he was a close friend.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: The good deed and the evil deed are not alike. Repel the evil deed with one which is better, then lo! he, between whom and thee there was enmity (will become) as though he was a bosom friend.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Good and evil [conduct] are not equal. Repel [evil] with what is best. [If you do so,] he between whom and you was enmity, will then be as though he were a sympathetic friend.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Good and evil deeds are not equal. Repel with that which is most just, and see, the one whom there is enmity between you will be as if he were a loyal guide.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And not equal are the good deed and the bad. Repel [evil] by that [deed] which is better; and thereupon the one whom between you and him is enmity [will become] as though he was a devoted friend.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Virtue and evil are not equal. If you replace evil habits by virtuous ones, you will certainly find that your enemies will become your intimate friends.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And not alike are the good and the evil. Repel (evil) with what is best, when lo! he between whom and you was enmity would be as if he were a warm friend.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Good and evil deeds are not equal. Repel evil with what is better; then you will see that one who was once your enemy has become your dearest friend,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Nor can goodness and Evil be equal. Repel (Evil) with what is better: Then will he between whom and thee was hatred become as it were thy friend and intimate!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wala tastawee alhasanatu wala alssayyiatu idfaAA biallatee hiya ahsanu faitha allathee baynaka wabaynahu AAadawatun kaannahu waliyyun hameemun

35.arabic: وَمَا يُلَقَّىٰهَآ إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ صَبَرُوا۟ وَمَا يُلَقَّىٰهَآ إِلَّا ذُو حَظٍّ عَظِيمٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Only they attain it who forbear, and only a man of great good fortune can achieve it.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And none receive this great treasure except those who are patient; and none receives this except one who is extremely fortunate.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Yet none shall receive it, except the steadfast; none shall receive it, except a man of mighty fortune.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And none attaineth that except those who are patient; and none attaineth that except the owner of mighty good fortune,

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: But none is granted it (the above quality) except those who are patient, and none is granted it except the owner of the great portion (of the happiness in the Hereafter i.e. Paradise and in this world of a high moral character).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: But none will attain it except those who persevere, and none will attain it except the very fortunate.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: But none attains to this except those who are steadfast; none attains to this except those endowed with mighty good fortune.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: But none is granted it except those who are patient -- and none is granted it except the owner of the great portion in this world.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: But none is granted it save those who are steadfast, and none is granted it save the owner of great happiness.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: But none is granted it except those who are patient, and none is granted it except the greatly endowed.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: But none will receive it except those who are patient and, none shall receive it, except he who has a great share.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But none is granted it except those who are patient, and none is granted it except one having a great portion [of good].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Only those who exercise patience and who have been granted a great share of God's favor can find such an opportunity.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And none are made to receive it but those who are patient, and none are made to receive it but those who have a mighty good fortune.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: but no one will be granted such goodness except those who exercise patience and self-restraint -- no one is granted it save those who are truly fortunate.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And no one will be granted such goodness except those who exercise patience and self-restraint,- none but persons of the greatest good fortune.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wama yulaqqaha illa allatheena sabaroo wama yulaqqaha illa thoo haththin AAatheemin

36.arabic: وَإِمَّا يَنزَغَنَّكَ مِنَ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ نَزْغٌ فَٱسْتَعِذْ بِٱللَّهِ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: If the Devil incite you to evil, seek refuge in God. He is all-hearing and all-knowing.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And O listener! If a distracting thought from the devil reaches you, seek the refuge of Allah; indeed He is the All Hearing, the All Knowing.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: If a provocation from Satan should provoke thee, seek refuge in God; He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And if there inciteth thee an incitement from the Satan then seek refuge in Allah. Verily He! He is the Hearer, the Knower.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And if an evil whisper from Shaitan (Satan) tries to turn you away (O Muhammad SAW) (from doing good, etc.), then seek refuge in Allah. Verily, He is the All-Hearer, the All-Knower.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: When a temptation from the Devil provokes you, seek refuge in God; He is the Hearer, the Knower.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And if you are prompted by a provocation from Satan, seek refuge with Allah. He, and He alone, is All-Hearing, All- Knowing.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And if an evil whisper from Shaytan tries to turn you away, then seek refuge in Allah. Verily, He is the All-Hearer, the All-Knower.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And if a whisper from the devil reach thee (O Muhammad) then seek refuge in Allah. Lo! He is the Hearer, the Knower.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Should an incitement from Satan prompt you [to ill feeling], seek the protection of Allah. Indeed, He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: If a provocation from satan should provoke you, seek refuge in Allah. He is the Hearer, the Knower.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And if there comes to you from Satan an evil suggestion, then seek refuge in Allah. Indeed, He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), seek God's protection if satan's temptation grieves you, for God is All-hearing and All-knowing.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And if an interference of the Shaitan should cause you mischief, seek refuge in Allah; surely He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: If a prompting from Satan should stir you, seek refuge with God: He is the All Hearing and the All Knowing.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And if (at any time) an incitement to discord is made to thee by the Evil One, seek refuge in Allah. He is the One Who hears and knows all things.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waimma yanzaghannaka mina alshshaytani nazghun faistaAAith biAllahi innahu huwa alssameeAAu alAAaleemu

37.arabic: وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِ ٱلَّيْلُ وَٱلنَّهَارُ وَٱلشَّمْسُ وَٱلْقَمَرُ ۚ لَا تَسْجُدُوا۟ لِلشَّمْسِ وَلَا لِلْقَمَرِ وَٱسْجُدُوا۟ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَهُنَّ إِن كُنتُمْ إِيَّاهُ تَعْبُدُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The night and day and the sun and moon are (only) some of His signs. So do not bow before the sun and the moon, but bow in homage to God who created them, if you truly worship Him.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And the night, and the day, and the sun, and the moon are among His signs; do not prostrate for the sun or the moon, but prostrate for Allah Who has created them, if you are His bondmen.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And of His signs are the night and the day, the sun and the moon. Bow not yourselves to the sun and moon, but bow yourselves to God who created them, if Him you serve.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And of His signs are the night and the day and the sun and the moon. So prostrate not yourselves Unto the sun nor the moon. but prostrate yourselves Unto Allah who hath created them, if it is He alone whom ye are worshipping.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And from among His Signs are the night and the day, and the sun and the moon. Prostrate not to the sun nor to the moon, but prostrate to Allah Who created them, if you (really) worship Him.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And of His signs are the night and the day, and the sun and the moon. Do not bow down to the sun, nor to the moon, but bow down to God, Who created them both, if it is Him that you serve.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And of His Signs are the night and the day, and the sun and the moon. Do not prostrate yourselves before the sun, nor before the moon, but prostrate yourselves before Allah Who created them, if it is Him that you serve.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And from among His signs are the night and the day, and the sun and the moon. Do not prostrate yourselves to the sun nor to the moon, but prostrate yourselves to Allah Who created them, if you worship Him.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And of His portents are the night and the day and the sun and the moon. Do not prostrate to the sun or the moon; but prostrate to Allah Who created them, if it is in truth Him Whom ye worship.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Among His signs are night and day, and the sun and the moon. Do not prostrate to the sun, nor to the moon, but prostrate to Allah who created them, if it is Him that you worship.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Among His signs are the night and the day, and the sun and the moon. But do not prostrate yourselves before the sun or the moon; rather prostrate before Allah, who created them both, if it is He whom you worship.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And of His signs are the night and day and the sun and moon. Do not prostrate to the sun or to the moon, but prostate to Allah, who created them, if it should be Him that you worship.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Some evidence of His existence) are the night, day, sun, and moon. Do not prostrate before the sun and the moon, but prostrate before God Who has created them if you want to worship Him alone.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And among His signs are the night and the day and the sun and the moon; do not prostrate to the sun nor to the moon; and prostrate to Allah Who created them, if Him it is that you serve.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Among His signs are the night and the day, and the sun and the moon. Do not prostrate yourselves before the sun and the moon, but prostrate yourselves before God who created them all, if it is truly Him that you worship.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Among His Signs are the Night and the Day, and the Sun and the Moon. Do not prostrate to the sun and the moon, but prostrate to Allah, Who created them, if it is Him ye wish to serve.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wamin ayatihi allaylu waalnnaharu waalshshamsu waalqamaru la tasjudoo lilshshamsi wala lilqamari waosjudoo lillahi alathee khalaqahunna in kuntum iyyahu taAAbudoona

38.arabic: فَإِنِ ٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ فَٱلَّذِينَ عِندَ رَبِّكَ يُسَبِّحُونَ لَهُۥ بِٱلَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ وَهُمْ لَا يَسْـَٔمُونَ ۩

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But if they become haughty (then remember) that those who are close to your Lord sing His praises night and day and do not grow weary of (doing so).

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So if these (disbelievers) be haughty, so (in any case) those (the angels) who are with your Lord say His Purity night and day, and they do not get weary. (Command of prostration # 11)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And if they wax proud, yet those who are with thy Lord do glorify Him by night and day, and grow not weary.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And if they grow stiff-necked, then verily those who are with thy Lord, hallow Him night and day, and they weary not.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: But if they are too proud (to do so), then there are those who are with your Lord (angels) glorify Him night and day, and never are they tired.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: But if they are too proud—those in the presence of your Lord praise Him night and day, and without ever tiring.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: But if they wax proud (and persist in their attitude, it does not matter, for) the angels near-stationed to your Lord glorify Him night and day, and never grow weary.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: But if they are too proud, then there are those who are with your Lord glorify Him night and day, and never are they tired.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: But if they are too proud - still those who are with thy Lord glorify Him night and day, and tire not.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: But if they disdain [the worship of Allah], those who are near your Lord glorify Him night and day, and they are not wearied.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: But if they become proud, those who are with your Lord exalt Him by day and night, and never grow weary.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But if they are arrogant - then those who are near your Lord exalt Him by night and by day, and they do not become weary.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: However, if people are too proud to prostrate before God, let them know that God's other creatures glorify Him both day and night without fatigue.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: But if they are proud, yet those with your Lord glorify Him during the night and the day, and they are not tired.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: If they grow arrogant, [remember that] those who are with your Lord glorify Him night and day and never grow tired.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But is the (Unbelievers) are arrogant, (no matter): for in the presence of thy Lord are those who celebrate His praises by night and by day. And they never flag (nor feel themselves above it).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faini istakbaroo faallatheena AAinda rabbika yusabbihoona lahu biallayli waalnnahari wahum la yasamoona

39.arabic: وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦٓ أَنَّكَ تَرَى ٱلْأَرْضَ خَـٰشِعَةً فَإِذَآ أَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْهَا ٱلْمَآءَ ٱهْتَزَّتْ وَرَبَتْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَحْيَاهَا لَمُحْىِ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰٓ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: It is among His signs that the earth you see all barren and desolate begins to stir and sprout when We send down rain upon it. Surely He who gives it life will also give life to the dead. Indeed He has power over everything.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And among His signs is that you see the earth lying neglected, so when We sent down water on it, it freshened up and grew forth; indeed He Who gave it life, will revive the dead; indeed He is Able to do all things.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And of His signs is that thou seest the earth humble; then, when We send down water upon it, it quivers, and swells. Surely He who quickens it is He who quickens the dead; surely He is powerful over everything.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And of His signs is that thou beholdest the earth lowly, and when We send down thereon water, it stirreth to life and groweth. Verily He who quickeneth is the Quickener of the dead. Verily He is over everything Potent.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And among His Signs (in this), that you see the earth barren, but when We send down water (rain) to it, it is stirred to life and growth (of vegetations). Verily, He Who gives it life, surely, (He) is Able to give life to the dead (on the Day of Resurrection). Indeed! He is Able to do all things.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And of His signs is that you see the land still. But when We send down water upon it, it stirs and grows. Surely, He Who revived it will revive the dead. He is Able to do all things.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And of His Signs is that you see the earth withered, then We send down water upon it, and lo! it quivers and swells. Surely He Who gives life to the dead earth will also give life to the dead. Surely He has power over everything.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And among His signs; that you see the earth barren, but when We send down water to it, it is stirred to life and growth. Verily, He Who gives it life, surely is able to give life to the dead. Indeed He is Able to do all things.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And of His portents (is this): that thou seest the earth lowly, but when We send down water thereon it thrilleth and groweth. Lo! He Who quickeneth it is verily the Quickener of the Dead. Lo! He is Able to do all things.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Among His signs, is that you see the earth desolate; but when We send down water upon it, it stirs and swells. Indeed, He who revives it will also revive the dead. Indeed, He has power over all things.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And among His signs is that you see the earth humble; then when He sends down rain upon it, it quivers and swells. He who revives is He who revives the dead, surely, He is powerful over everything.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And of His signs is that you see the earth stilled, but when We send down upon it rain, it quivers and grows. Indeed, He who has given it life is the Giver of Life to the dead. Indeed, He is over all things competent.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Further evidence is that (at times) you find the earth to be barren. When it is watered it moves and swells (to let the plants grow). The One who brings it back to life will also bring the dead back to life. He has power over all things.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And among His signs is this, that you see the earth still, but when We send down on it the water, it stirs and swells: most surely He Who gives it life is the Giver of life to the dead; surely He has power over all things.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Among His signs is this: you see the earth dry and barren, but when We send down on it water, it stirs and swells: most surely He who gives it life is the giver of life to the dead; surely He has power over all things.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And among His Signs in this: thou seest the earth barren and desolate; but when We send down rain to it, it is stirred to life and yields increase. Truly, He Who gives life to the (dead) earth can surely give life to (men) who are dead. For He has power over all things.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wamin ayatihi annaka tara alarda khashiAAatan faitha anzalna AAalayha almaa ihtazzat warabat inna allathee ahyaha lamuhyee almawta innahu AAala kulli shayin qadeerun

40.arabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يُلْحِدُونَ فِىٓ ءَايَـٰتِنَا لَا يَخْفَوْنَ عَلَيْنَآ ۗ أَفَمَن يُلْقَىٰ فِى ٱلنَّارِ خَيْرٌ أَم مَّن يَأْتِىٓ ءَامِنًا يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۚ ٱعْمَلُوا۟ مَا شِئْتُمْ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Surely those who slander Our signs are not hidden from Us. Then, is he better who will be cast into Hell, or he who will come out safe on the Day of Resurrection? Do whatever you will, He sees whatsoever you do.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Indeed those who distort Our verses are not hidden from Us; so is one who is cast into the fire better, or one who comes in safety on the Day of Resurrection? Do whatever you wish! He is indeed seeing your deeds.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Those who blaspheme Our signs are not hidden from Us. What, is he who shall be cast into the Fire better, or he who comes on the Day of Resurrection in security? Do what you will; surely He sees the things you do.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily those who blaspheme Our revelations are not hid from Us. Is he then who will be cast into the Fire better or he who cometh secure on the Day of Resurrection? Work whatsoever ye will; Verily He is of that which ye work the Beholder.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily, those who turn away from Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc. by attacking, distorting and denying them), are not hidden from Us. Is he who is cast into the Fire better or he who comes secure on the Day of Resurrection? Do what you will. Verily! He is All-Seer of what you do (this is a severe threat to the disbelievers).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those who despise Our revelations are not hidden from Us. Is he who is hurled into the Fire better? Or he who arrives safely on the Day of Resurrection? Do as you please; He is Seeing of everything you do.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Those who pervert Our Signs are not hidden from Us. Is he who will be cast into the Fire better, or he who comes secure on the Day of Resurrection? Do as you wish; He sees all what you do.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, those who Yulhiduna Fi Our Ayat are not hidden from Us. Is he who is cast into the Fire better or he who comes secure on the Day of Resurrection Do what you will. Verily, He is All-Seer of what you do.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! those who distort Our revelations are not hid from Us. Is he who is hurled into the Fire better, or he who cometh secure on the Day of Resurrection? Do what ye will. Lo! He is Seer of what ye do.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Those who abuse Our signs are not hidden from Us. Is someone who is cast in the Fire better off, or someone who arrives safely on the Day of Resurrection? Act as you wish; indeed He watches what you do.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Those who disbelieve Our verses when it comes to them are not hidden from Us. Is he who is cast in the Fire better than he who comes in safety on the Day of Resurrection? Do as you will, surely, He sees the things you do.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, those who inject deviation into Our verses are not concealed from Us. So, is he who is cast into the Fire better or he who comes secure on the Day of Resurrection? Do whatever you will; indeed, He is Seeing of what you do.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Those who deviate from Our revelations are not hidden from Us. Is the one who will be thrown into hell fire better than the one who will be brought safely into the presence of God on the Day of Judgment? Act as you wish; God is Well-Aware of whatever you do.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Surely they who deviate from the right way concerning Our communications are not hidden from Us. What! is he then who is cast into the fire better, or he who comes safe on the day of resurrection? Do what you like, surely He sees what you do.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Those who distort the meaning of Our message are not concealed from Us. Who is better -- someone who will be thrown into the Fire or someone who will arrive in safety on the Day of Resurrection? Do as you will, He sees whatever you do.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Those who pervert the Truth in Our Signs are not hidden from Us. Which is better?- he that is cast into the Fire, or he that comes safe through, on the Day of Judgment? Do what ye will: verily He seeth (clearly) all that ye do.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna allatheena yulhidoona fee ayatina la yakhfawna AAalayna afaman yulqa fee alnnari khayrun am man yatee aminan yawma alqiyamati iAAmaloo ma shitum innahu bima taAAmaloona baseerun

41.arabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِٱلذِّكْرِ لَمَّا جَآءَهُمْ ۖ وَإِنَّهُۥ لَكِتَـٰبٌ عَزِيزٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Those who reject the Reminder when it has come to them (should know) that it is a Book inviolate.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Indeed those who denied the Remembrance when it came to them – they are truly ruined; and indeed it is an honourable Book.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Those who disbelieve in the Remembrance when it comes to them -- and surely it is a Book Sublime;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily those who disbelieve in the Admonition when it cometh Unto them are themselves to blame. Verily it is a Book mighty.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily, those who disbelieved in the Reminder (i.e. the Quran) when it came to them (shall receive the punishment). And verily, it is an honourable respected Book (because it is Allah's Speech, and He has protected it from corruption, etc.). (See V. 15:9]

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those who reject the Reminder when it has come to them—it is an invincible Book.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: These are the ones who rejected the Good Counsel when it came to them, although it is certainly a Mighty Book.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, those who disbelieved in the Reminder when it came to them. And verily, it is an honorable well-fortified respected Book.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! those who disbelieve in the Reminder when it cometh unto them (are guilty), for lo! it is an unassailable Scripture.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Those who reject the Reminder when it comes to them... Indeed, it is an august Book:

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Those who disbelieve in the Remembrance when it comes to them and indeed this is a Mighty Book

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, those who disbelieve in the message after it has come to them... And indeed, it is a mighty Book.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The disbelievers (do not know) that the Quran which was sent to them is certainly a glorious Book.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Surely those who disbelieve in the reminder when it comes to them, and most surely it is a Mighty Book:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Those who reject the Reminder [the Quran] when it comes to them [are the losers] -- truly it is a mighty Book:

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Those who reject the Message when it comes to them (are not hidden from Us). And indeed it is a Book of exalted power.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna allatheena kafaroo bialththikri lamma jaahum wainnahu lakitabun AAazeezun

42.arabic: لَّا يَأْتِيهِ ٱلْبَـٰطِلُ مِنۢ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَلَا مِنْ خَلْفِهِۦ ۖ تَنزِيلٌ مِّنْ حَكِيمٍ حَمِيدٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Falsehood cannot enter it from any side: It's a revelation from the all-wise and praiseworthy (God).

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Falsehood cannot approach it – neither from its front nor from its back; it is sent down by the Wise, the Most Praiseworthy.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: falsehood comes not to it from before it nor from behind it; a sending down from One All-wise, All-laudable.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Falsehood cannot come at it from before it or behind it It, a revelation from One Wise and Praiseworthy.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Falsehood cannot come to it from before it or behind it (it is) sent down by the All-Wise, Worthy of all praise (Allah).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Falsehood cannot approach it, from before it or behind it. It is a revelation from One Wise and Praiseworthy.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Falsehood may not enter it from the front or from the rear. It is a revelation that has been sent down from the Most Wise, the Immensely Praiseworthy.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Falsehood cannot come to it from before it or behind it, (it is) sent down by the All-Wise, Worthy of all praise.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Falsehood cannot come at it from before it or from behind it. (It is) a revelation from the Wise, the Owner of Praise.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: falsehood cannot approach it, at present or in future, a [revelation gradually] sent down from One all-wise, all-laudable.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: falsehood does not come to it from before it or from behind it. It is a sending down from the One, the Wise, the Praised.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Falsehood cannot approach it from before it or from behind it; [it is] a revelation from a [Lord who is] Wise and Praiseworthy.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Falsehood can not reach it from any direction. It is the revelation from the All-wise, Praiseworthy One.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Falsehood shall not come to it from before it nor from behind it; a revelation from the Wise, the Praised One.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: falsehood shall not reach from before or from behind. It is a revelation from the Wise, the One worthy of all praise.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: No falsehood can approach it from before or behind it: It is sent down by One Full of Wisdom, Worthy of all Praise.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: La yateehi albatilu min bayni yadayhi wala min khalfihi tanzeelun min hakeemin hameedin

43.arabic: مَّا يُقَالُ لَكَ إِلَّا مَا قَدْ قِيلَ لِلرُّسُلِ مِن قَبْلِكَ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَذُو مَغْفِرَةٍ وَذُو عِقَابٍ أَلِيمٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Nothing is said to you which had not been said to other apostles before you. Surely your Lord is the lord of forgiveness, but also the lord of severe retribution.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: You will not be told except what was said to the Noble Messengers before you; that “Your Lord is the Owner of Forgiveness, and the Owner of Painful Punishment.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Naught is said to thee but what already was said to the Messengers before thee. Surely thy Lord is a Lord of forgiveness and of painful retribution.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Naught is said Unto thee save that which was said Unto the apostles afore thee. Verily thy Lord is Owner of forgiveness and Owner of afflictive chastisement.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Nothing is said to you (O Muhammad SAW) except what was said to the Messengers before you. Verily, your Lord is the Possessor of forgiveness, and (also) the Possessor of painful punishment.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Nothing is said to you but was said to the Messengers before you: your Lord is Possessor of Forgiveness, and Possessor of Painful Repayment.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (O Prophet), nothing is said to you but what was already said to the Messengers before you. Surely your Lord is the Lord of forgiveness and the Lord of grievous chastisement.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Nothing is said to you except what was said to the Messengers before you. Verily, your Lord is the Possessor of forgiveness, and (also) the Possessor of painful punishment.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Naught is said unto thee (Muhammad) save what was said unto the messengers before thee. Lo! thy Lord is owner of forgiveness, and owner (also) of dire punishment.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Nothing is said to you except what has already been said to the apostles before you. Indeed your Lord is One who forgives and also metes out a painful retribution.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Nothing that is said to you has not already been said to other Messengers before you. Indeed, your Lord is a Lord of forgiveness, but stern in retribution.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Nothing is said to you, [O Muhammad], except what was already said to the messengers before you. Indeed, your Lord is a possessor of forgiveness and a possessor of painful penalty.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Nothing has been said to you which was not said to the Messengers who lived before you. Your Lord is certainly All-forgiving, but stern in His retribution.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Naught is said to you but what was said indeed to the apostles before you; surely your Lord is the Lord of forgiveness and the Lord of painful retribution.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Nothing is said to you but what was said indeed to the messengers before you; surely your Lord is the Lord of forgiveness but also the Lord of painful retribution.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Nothing is said to thee that was not said to the messengers before thee: that thy lord has at his Command (all) forgiveness as well as a most Grievous Penalty.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ma yuqalu laka illa ma qad qeela lilrrusuli min qablika inna rabbaka lathoo maghfiratin wathoo AAiqabin aleemin

44.arabic: وَلَوْ جَعَلْنَـٰهُ قُرْءَانًا أَعْجَمِيًّا لَّقَالُوا۟ لَوْلَا فُصِّلَتْ ءَايَـٰتُهُۥٓ ۖ ءَا۬عْجَمِىٌّ وَعَرَبِىٌّ ۗ قُلْ هُوَ لِلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ هُدًى وَشِفَآءٌ ۖ وَٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ فِىٓ ءَاذَانِهِمْ وَقْرٌ وَهُوَ عَلَيْهِمْ عَمًى ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ يُنَادَوْنَ مِن مَّكَانٍۭ بَعِيدٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: If We had made it a discourse in an obscure tongue, they would have said: "Why were its revelations not expounded distinctly? A foreign tongue and an Arab (audience)?" Say: "For those who believe it is a guidance and a healing; but for those who do not believe it is a deafness in the ears, and a blindness. They are those one calls to from far away.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And if We had made it as a Qur’an in a foreign language they would have certainly said, “Why were its verses not explained in detail?” What! The Book in a foreign language, and the Prophet an Arab?! Proclaim (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “It is a guidance and a cure for the believers”; and there is deafness in the ears of those who do not believe, and it is blindness upon them; as if they are being called from a place far away!

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: If We had made it a barbarous Koran, they would have said, 'Why are its signs not distinguished? What, barbarous and Arabic?' Say: 'To the believers it is a guidance, and a healing; but those who believe not, in their ears is a heaviness, and to them it is a blindness; those -- they are called from a far place.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And had We made it a recital in a foreign tongue, they would have surely said: wherefore are the verses thereof not detailed? A foreign tongue and an Arab! Say thou: Unto those who have believed it is a guidance and a healing: and those who believe not, - in their ears there is a heaviness, and Unto them it is blindness. These are they who are cried Unto from a place far-off.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And if We had sent this as a Quran in a foreign language other than Arabic, they would have said: "Why are not its Verses explained in detail (in our language)? What! (A Book) not in Arabic and (the Messenger) an Arab?" Say: "It is for those who believe, a guide and a healing. And as for those who disbelieve, there is heaviness (deafness) in their ears, and it (the Quran) is blindness for them. They are those who are called from a place far away (so they neither listen nor understand).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Had We made it a Quran in a foreign language, they would have said, “If only its verses were made clear.” Non-Arabic and an Arab? Say, “For those who believe, it is guidance and healing. But as for those who do not believe: there is heaviness in their ears, and it is blindness for them. These are being called from a distant place.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Had We revealed this as a non-Arabic Qur'an they would have said: “Why were its verses not clearly expounded? How strange, a non-Arabic scripture and an Arab audience!” Tell them: “It is a guidance and a healing to the believers. But to those who do not believe, it serves as a plug in their ears and a covering over their eyes. It is as if they are being called from a place far away.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And if We had sent this as a Qur'an in a foreign language, they would have said: "Why are not its verses explained in detail What! Not in Arabic nor an Arab" Say: "It is for those who believe, a guide and a cure. And as for those who disbelieve, there is heaviness in their ears, and it is blindness for them. They are called from a place far away."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And if We had appointed it a Lecture in a foreign tongue they would assuredly have said: If only its verses were expounded (so that we might understand)? What! A foreign tongue and an Arab? - Say unto them (O Muhammad): For those who believe it is a guidance and a healing; and as for those who disbelieve, there is a deafness in their ears, and it is blindness for them. Such are called to from afar.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Had We made it a non-Arabic Quran, they would have said, ‘Why have not its signs been articulated?’ ‘What! A non-Arabian [scripture] and an Arabian [prophet]!?’ Say, ‘It is guidance and healing for those who have faith. As for those who are faithless, there is a deafness in their ears and it is lost to their sight: [to them it is as if] they were called from a distant place.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Had We made the Koran in a nonArabic (language) they would have said: 'If only its verses were distinguished! Why in (a) nonArabic (language, when theProphet is) an Arab' Say: 'To the believers it is a guidance and a healing. But to those who do not believe, there is a heaviness in their ears, to them it is blindness. They are those called from afar'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And if We had made it a non-Arabic Qur'an, they would have said, "Why are its verses not explained in detail [in our language]? Is it a foreign [recitation] and an Arab [messenger]?" Say, "It is, for those who believe, a guidance and cure." And those who do not believe - in their ears is deafness, and it is upon them blindness. Those are being called from a distant place.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Had We sent down this Quran in a non-Arabic language, they would have said, "Why have its verses not been well expounded?"
